1	# Documentation Frontmatter Schema
2	
3	This file defines the YAML frontmatter schema used for both interim and canonical design documentation. The two schemas share most fields; canonical adds fields that require taxonomy decisions you'll defer until the decomposition pass.
4	
5	## Why one combined schema
6	
7	Interim docs are produced now, during the period before the major doc decomposition. They will eventually be merged into the canonical structure. Keeping interim and canonical schemas aligned means the merge is mostly additive (filling in canonical-only fields on existing interim docs) rather than transformative.
8	
9	## Fields used by both interim and canonical docs
10	
11	```yaml
12	---
13	# IDENTITY
14	id: feature-or-area-slug          # kebab-case, unique across all docs, stable
15	title: Human-Readable Title       # display title
16	
17	# STATUS
18	status: interim                   # interim | canonical | deprecated | draft
19	created: 2026-05-15               # ISO 8601 date
20	last_updated: 2026-05-15          # ISO 8601 date, updated on every change
21	last_synced_with_code: 2026-05-15 # ISO 8601 date, updated when code-doc reconciliation runs
22	
23	# PROVENANCE
24	work_units:                       # list of work-unit log filenames that contributed to this doc
25	  - 2026-05-12_phase_03_extract_renderer_core
26	  - 2026-05-14_phase_04_apply_constraints
27	
28	# IMPLEMENTATION
29	implementation:                   # list of code file paths this doc describes
30	  - src/renderer/core.ts
31	  - src/renderer/constraints.ts
32	  - src/main.rs:1245-1289         # line ranges are OK and helpful
33	
34	# RELATIONSHIPS
35	related:                          # informal list of related doc IDs (used by interim)
36	  - other-feature-slug
37	  - another-area-slug
38	---
39	```
40	
41	## Additional fields for canonical docs only
42	
43	These are populated during the decomposition pass when you have a stable taxonomy. Leave them out of interim docs.
44	
45	```yaml
46	---
47	# TAXONOMY (canonical only)
48	category: ui-pattern              # see "Canonical categories" below
49	scope: app-wide                   # app-wide | module-specific | service | data | api
50	
51	# WHERE IT APPLIES (canonical only)
52	applies_to:
53	  - all-modules                   # or: list of specific module IDs
54	  - frontend                      # or: backend | data | infra
55	
56	# FORMAL CROSS-REFERENCES (canonical only)
57	# These replace the informal `related` field
58	references:                       # docs this one depends on
59	  - design-tokens
60	  - context-awareness
61	referenced_by:                    # docs that depend on this one
62	  - module-designer
63	  - module-console
64	  - module-dashboard
65	
66	# CANONICAL PATTERN MARKERS (canonical only)
67	is_canonical_pattern: true        # if true, this is the SSOT for this concept
68	canonical_pattern: parent-slug    # if this is an instance, points to the canonical pattern
69	
70	# IMPLEMENTATION EXAMPLES (canonical only)
71	canonical_examples:               # specific code locations that demonstrate the pattern
72	  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts:42-78
73	---
74	```
75	
76	## Canonical categories
77	
78	When you get to the canonical pass, these are the suggested top-level categories. Adjust as the project's actual shape becomes clear.
79	
80	- `ui-pattern` — reusable interaction or visual patterns (right-click menus, modals, tooltips)
81	- `module` — a major functional area of the app (designer, dashboard, reports)
82	- `service` — a backend service or capability (auth, persistence, eventing)
83	- `data` — data structures, schemas, storage formats
84	- `api` — internal or external API surfaces
85	- `infra` — build, deploy, configuration concerns
86	- `design-token` — visual or behavioral tokens shared across the app
87	
88	Each category implies different expected fields. Modules describe themselves; ui-patterns describe behavior; data describes shape; api describes contract.
89	
90	## Field rules
91	
92	### `id`
93	- Lowercase, kebab-case, no spaces, no special characters except hyphens
94	- Globally unique across all docs in the project
95	- Stable: do not rename. If a concept name changes substantially, create a new doc and mark the old one `status: deprecated` with a pointer.
96	
97	### `status`
98	- `interim` — produced during the pre-decomposition period; will be merged into canonical structure
99	- `canonical` — part of the canonical structure after decomposition; SSOT for its concept
100	- `deprecated` — kept for historical reference; pointers redirect readers/agents to the replacement
101	- `draft` — incomplete, not yet authoritative
102	
103	### Dates
104	- All dates ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`)
105	- `created` set once and never updated
106	- `last_updated` updated every time the file is meaningfully modified
107	- `last_synced_with_code` updated when the doc has been verified against current code reality
108	
109	### `implementation`
110	- List of paths relative to project root
111	- Line ranges welcome (`src/file.ts:42-78`)
112	- Order roughly from most-canonical to least-canonical example
113	- Empty list is acceptable for purely conceptual docs (no specific code yet)
114	
115	### `work_units`
116	- Filenames of work-unit logs that contributed to this doc, without `.md` extension
117	- Append-only — each new work unit that updates this doc adds to the list
118	- Enables tracing any documentation claim back to the conversation that produced it
119	
120	### `related` (interim) vs `references`/`referenced_by` (canonical)
121	- During the interim period, just maintain `related` as an informal list
122	- During canonical decomposition, `related` is replaced with `references` and `referenced_by`, which are bidirectional and formally maintained
123	
124	## Body content conventions
125	
126	Below the frontmatter, the document body uses these conventions:
127	
128	- First-level heading is the title (matches `title` field)
129	- Brief 1-3 sentence summary directly under the title
130	- Sections use second-level headings (`##`)
131	- Standard section order when applicable:
132	  1. Purpose / Overview
133	  2. Behavior
134	  3. Caveats
135	  4. Implementation Notes
136	  5. Changelog (interim docs use a changelog section; see below)
137	
138	## Changelog section (interim docs only)
139	
140	Interim docs include a changelog section at the bottom of the body. This satisfies the user-requested ability to roll back doc updates if a wrap-up sequence produces a wrong update.
141	
142	```markdown
143	## Changelog
144	
145	<!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
146	<!-- Each entry is appended on update. Older entries on bottom. Newer on top. -->
147	
148	### 2026-05-15
149	Updated implementation list to include constraints.ts. Refined caveats section based on findings in phase 4 work unit.
150	
151	### 2026-05-12
152	Initial creation from phase 3 work unit. Documented core renderer extraction.
153	```
154	
155	The HTML comments tell the LLM to skip the changelog during normal reads. When you specifically want to audit history, you can ask the LLM to read the changelog section.
156	
157	Canonical docs do not need this section — git history serves that role once docs are stable.
158	
159	## Examples
160	
161	### Minimal interim doc
162	
163	```markdown
164	---
165	id: shape-resize-sidecar-positioning
166	title: Shape Resize Sidecar Positioning
167	status: interim
168	created: 2026-05-15
169	last_updated: 2026-05-15
170	last_synced_with_code: 2026-05-15
171	work_units:
172	  - 2026-05-15_fix_sidecar_scaling
173	implementation:
174	  - src/designer/shapes/scaleHandler.ts
175	related:
176	  - designer-resize-controls
177	  - sidecar-rendering
178	---
179	
180	# Shape Resize Sidecar Positioning
181	
182	When a shape is resized, sidecars maintain their absolute pixel offsets from the shape's edges and center rather than scaling along with the shape.
183	
184	## Behavior
185	
186	Sidecar offsets are measured from the resized shape's edges. If a sidecar's left edge is 10px right of the shape's right edge, that remains true at any shape size.
187	
188	## Caveats
189	
190	The interior fill gauge is the one exception — it scales with the shape.
191	
192	## Implementation Notes
193	
194	The fix lives in the resize handler's preview-update path. The final-commit path was already correct; only the live preview was incorrectly scaling sidecar offsets.
195	
196	## Changelog
197	
198	<!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
199	
200	### 2026-05-15
201	Initial creation from the sidecar scaling fix work unit.
202	```
203	
204	### Eventual canonical doc (after decomposition)
205	
206	```markdown
207	---
208	id: ui-right-click-menus
209	title: Right-Click Menus
210	status: canonical
211	created: 2026-05-15
212	last_updated: 2026-08-01
213	last_synced_with_code: 2026-08-01
214	category: ui-pattern
215	scope: app-wide
216	applies_to:
217	  - all-modules
218	  - frontend
219	is_canonical_pattern: true
220	work_units:
221	  - 2026-05-15_initial_decomposition
222	  - 2026-06-22_module_designer_context_menus
223	references:
224	  - design-tokens
225	  - context-awareness
226	referenced_by:
227	  - module-designer
228	  - module-console
229	  - module-dashboard
230	implementation:
231	  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts
232	  - src/main.rs:1245-1289
233	canonical_examples:
234	  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts:42-78
235	---
236	
237	# Right-Click Menus
238	
239	The application intercepts right-click events globally. Menus render at the click position and are always context-aware.
240	
241	## Behavior
242	
243	[...]
244	
245	## Caveats
246	
247	In the designer module, right-click in test mode behaves differently than in design mode — see `module-designer-test-mode`.
248	
249	## Implementation Notes
250	
251	The canonical implementation lives in `src/menus/contextMenu.ts`. When implementing right-click handling in a new module, replicate this pattern rather than creating a new one.
252	```

# Documentation Frontmatter Schema

This file defines the YAML frontmatter schema used for both interim and canonical design documentation. The two schemas share most fields; canonical adds fields that require taxonomy decisions you'll defer until the decomposition pass.

## Why one combined schema

Interim docs are produced now, during the period before the major doc decomposition. They will eventually be merged into the canonical structure. Keeping interim and canonical schemas aligned means the merge is mostly additive (filling in canonical-only fields on existing interim docs) rather than transformative.

## Fields used by both interim and canonical docs

```yaml
---
# IDENTITY
id: feature-or-area-slug          # kebab-case, unique across all docs, stable
title: Human-Readable Title       # display title

# STATUS
status: interim                   # interim | canonical | deprecated | draft
created: 2026-05-15               # ISO 8601 date
last_updated: 2026-05-15          # ISO 8601 date, updated on every change
last_synced_with_code: 2026-05-15 # ISO 8601 date, updated when code-doc reconciliation runs

# PROVENANCE
work_units:                       # list of work-unit log filenames that contributed to this doc
  - 2026-05-12_phase_03_extract_renderer_core
  - 2026-05-14_phase_04_apply_constraints

# IMPLEMENTATION
implementation:                   # list of code file paths this doc describes
  - src/renderer/core.ts
  - src/renderer/constraints.ts
  - src/main.rs:1245-1289         # line ranges are OK and helpful

# RELATIONSHIPS
related:                          # informal list of related doc IDs (used by interim)
  - other-feature-slug
  - another-area-slug
---
```

## Additional fields for canonical docs only

These are populated during the decomposition pass when you have a stable taxonomy. Leave them out of interim docs.

```yaml
---
# TAXONOMY (canonical only)
category: ui-pattern              # see "Canonical categories" below
scope: app-wide                   # app-wide | module-specific | service | data | api

# WHERE IT APPLIES (canonical only)
applies_to:
  - all-modules                   # or: list of specific module IDs
  - frontend                      # or: backend | data | infra

# FORMAL CROSS-REFERENCES (canonical only)
# These replace the informal `related` field
references:                       # docs this one depends on
  - design-tokens
  - context-awareness
referenced_by:                    # docs that depend on this one
  - module-designer
  - module-console
  - module-dashboard

# CANONICAL PATTERN MARKERS (canonical only)
is_canonical_pattern: true        # if true, this is the SSOT for this concept
canonical_pattern: parent-slug    # if this is an instance, points to the canonical pattern

# IMPLEMENTATION EXAMPLES (canonical only)
canonical_examples:               # specific code locations that demonstrate the pattern
  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts:42-78
---
```

## Canonical categories

When you get to the canonical pass, these are the suggested top-level categories. Adjust as the project's actual shape becomes clear.

- `ui-pattern` — reusable interaction or visual patterns (right-click menus, modals, tooltips)
- `module` — a major functional area of the app (designer, dashboard, reports)
- `service` — a backend service or capability (auth, persistence, eventing)
- `data` — data structures, schemas, storage formats
- `api` — internal or external API surfaces
- `infra` — build, deploy, configuration concerns
- `design-token` — visual or behavioral tokens shared across the app

Each category implies different expected fields. Modules describe themselves; ui-patterns describe behavior; data describes shape; api describes contract.

## Field rules

### `id`
- Lowercase, kebab-case, no spaces, no special characters except hyphens
- Globally unique across all docs in the project
- Stable: do not rename. If a concept name changes substantially, create a new doc and mark the old one `status: deprecated` with a pointer.

### `status`
- `interim` — produced during the pre-decomposition period; will be merged into canonical structure
- `canonical` — part of the canonical structure after decomposition; SSOT for its concept
- `deprecated` — kept for historical reference; pointers redirect readers/agents to the replacement
- `draft` — incomplete, not yet authoritative

### Dates
- All dates ISO 8601 (`YYYY-MM-DD`)
- `created` set once and never updated
- `last_updated` updated every time the file is meaningfully modified
- `last_synced_with_code` updated when the doc has been verified against current code reality

### `implementation`
- List of paths relative to project root
- Line ranges welcome (`src/file.ts:42-78`)
- Order roughly from most-canonical to least-canonical example
- Empty list is acceptable for purely conceptual docs (no specific code yet)

### `work_units`
- Filenames of work-unit logs that contributed to this doc, without `.md` extension
- Append-only — each new work unit that updates this doc adds to the list
- Enables tracing any documentation claim back to the conversation that produced it

### `related` (interim) vs `references`/`referenced_by` (canonical)
- During the interim period, just maintain `related` as an informal list
- During canonical decomposition, `related` is replaced with `references` and `referenced_by`, which are bidirectional and formally maintained

## Body content conventions

Below the frontmatter, the document body uses these conventions:

- First-level heading is the title (matches `title` field)
- Brief 1-3 sentence summary directly under the title
- Sections use second-level headings (`##`)
- Standard section order when applicable:
  1. Purpose / Overview
  2. Behavior
  3. Caveats
  4. Implementation Notes
  5. Changelog (interim docs use a changelog section; see below)

## Changelog section (interim docs only)

Interim docs include a changelog section at the bottom of the body. This satisfies the user-requested ability to roll back doc updates if a wrap-up sequence produces a wrong update.

```markdown
## Changelog

<!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
<!-- Each entry is appended on update. Older entries on bottom. Newer on top. -->

### 2026-05-15
Updated implementation list to include constraints.ts. Refined caveats section based on findings in phase 4 work unit.

### 2026-05-12
Initial creation from phase 3 work unit. Documented core renderer extraction.
```

The HTML comments tell the LLM to skip the changelog during normal reads. When you specifically want to audit history, you can ask the LLM to read the changelog section.

Canonical docs do not need this section — git history serves that role once docs are stable.

## Examples

### Minimal interim doc

```markdown
---
id: shape-resize-sidecar-positioning
title: Shape Resize Sidecar Positioning
status: interim
created: 2026-05-15
last_updated: 2026-05-15
last_synced_with_code: 2026-05-15
work_units:
  - 2026-05-15_fix_sidecar_scaling
implementation:
  - src/designer/shapes/scaleHandler.ts
related:
  - designer-resize-controls
  - sidecar-rendering
---

# Shape Resize Sidecar Positioning

When a shape is resized, sidecars maintain their absolute pixel offsets from the shape's edges and center rather than scaling along with the shape.

## Behavior

Sidecar offsets are measured from the resized shape's edges. If a sidecar's left edge is 10px right of the shape's right edge, that remains true at any shape size.

## Caveats

The interior fill gauge is the one exception — it scales with the shape.

## Implementation Notes

The fix lives in the resize handler's preview-update path. The final-commit path was already correct; only the live preview was incorrectly scaling sidecar offsets.

## Changelog

<!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->

### 2026-05-15
Initial creation from the sidecar scaling fix work unit.
```

### Eventual canonical doc (after decomposition)

```markdown
---
id: ui-right-click-menus
title: Right-Click Menus
status: canonical
created: 2026-05-15
last_updated: 2026-08-01
last_synced_with_code: 2026-08-01
category: ui-pattern
scope: app-wide
applies_to:
  - all-modules
  - frontend
is_canonical_pattern: true
work_units:
  - 2026-05-15_initial_decomposition
  - 2026-06-22_module_designer_context_menus
references:
  - design-tokens
  - context-awareness
referenced_by:
  - module-designer
  - module-console
  - module-dashboard
implementation:
  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts
  - src/main.rs:1245-1289
canonical_examples:
  - src/menus/contextMenu.ts:42-78
---

# Right-Click Menus

The application intercepts right-click events globally. Menus render at the click position and are always context-aware.

## Behavior

[...]

## Caveats

In the designer module, right-click in test mode behaves differently than in design mode — see `module-designer-test-mode`.

## Implementation Notes

The canonical implementation lives in `src/menus/contextMenu.ts`. When implementing right-click handling in a new module, replicate this pattern rather than creating a new one.
```

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# lib-common.sh — shared helpers for the workflow suite
3	#
4	# Sourced by other scripts in this suite. Do not execute directly.
5	#
6	# This file is intended to be reviewed and customized by your local Claude
7	# Code instance for your specific project. Comments are deliberately verbose
8	# during the integration phase; strip them once you've adapted to your setup.
9	
10	# ============================================================================
11	# CONFIGURATION
12	# ============================================================================
13	
14	# Project-local directories. All workflow state lives under .claude/
15	# These paths are relative to $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR which Claude Code sets.
16	export WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/logs"
17	export WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state"
18	export WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/summaries"
19	export WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/archive"
20	export WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/reviews"
21	export WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/docs/interim"
22	
23	# Where the hook scripts live (used by claude -p invocations that need to
24	# reference helper scripts). Adjust if you put scripts elsewhere.
25	export WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts"
26	
27	# Feature flags. Set to 0 to disable specific behaviors during wrap-up.
28	# Useful for cost control or while iterating on the suite.
29	export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY:-1}"
30	export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW:-1}"
31	export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS:-1}"
32	
33	# Timeout for claude -p invocations (seconds). Should be lower than the hook
34	# timeout so we surface our own timeout error instead of getting killed mid-run.
35	export WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT="${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT:-180}"
36	
37	# Tag detection patterns. These are the supported tags in user prompts.
38	# Square brackets, lowercase. If you want to add new tags, add them here AND
39	# add corresponding handling in stop.sh.
40	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT='\[initprompt\]'
41	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE='\[phaseprompt\]'
42	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP='\[wrapup\]'
43	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW='\[review\]'
44	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW='\[deepreview\]'
45	# docfresh has a parameter: [docfresh:slug-name]
46	export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH='\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]'
47	
48	# Idle threshold in seconds for auto-rotating the work-unit log if no tag
49	# triggers a rotation. 4 hours by default. Set to 0 to disable idle rotation.
50	export WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS="${WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS:-14400}"
51	
52	# ============================================================================
53	# DIRECTORY SETUP
54	# ============================================================================
55	
56	ensure_workflow_dirs() {
57	    mkdir -p "$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR" \
58	             "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR" \
59	             "$WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR" \
60	             "$WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR" \
61	             "$WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR" \
62	             "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"
63	}
64	
65	# ============================================================================
66	# LOGGING & ERROR HANDLING
67	# ============================================================================
68	
69	# Hook-internal logging. Writes to .claude/state/hook_debug.log
70	# Does NOT use stdout/stderr because those have specific meaning to Claude Code.
71	hook_debug() {
72	    local msg="$1"
73	    local hook_name="${HOOK_NAME:-unknown}"
74	    echo "$(date -Iseconds) [$hook_name] $msg" >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" 2>/dev/null || true
75	}
76	
77	# Emit a structured error that bubbles up to Claude so we don't silently fail.
78	# Per the user's requirement: when something fails, Claude needs to know.
79	# Use this for non-blocking errors (the hook ran but had a problem).
80	hook_error_to_claude() {
81	    local context="$1"
82	    local detail="$2"
83	    # additionalContext mechanism in hookSpecificOutput
84	    # The hook still exits 0 — we're using context injection, not blocking.
85	    jq -nc \
86	        --arg event "${CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT_NAME:-Unknown}" \
87	        --arg ctx "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR [$context]: $detail" \
88	        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: $event, additionalContext: $ctx}}'
89	}
90	
91	# Emit a fatal error and exit non-zero. Use sparingly — non-blocking events
92	# treat any non-zero exit as a non-blocking error message in the transcript,
93	# which is exactly what we want for visibility.
94	hook_fatal() {
95	    local msg="$1"
96	    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: $msg" >&2
97	    exit 1
98	}
99	
100	# ============================================================================
101	# TAG DETECTION
102	# ============================================================================
103	
104	# Reads a prompt string, returns 0 if it contains the given tag pattern, 1 otherwise.
105	prompt_has_tag() {
106	    local prompt="$1"
107	    local tag_pattern="$2"
108	    echo "$prompt" | grep -qE "$tag_pattern"
109	}
110	
111	# Extracts a docfresh slug from a prompt: [docfresh:my-slug] -> my-slug
112	extract_docfresh_slug() {
113	    local prompt="$1"
114	    echo "$prompt" | grep -oE '\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]' | head -1 | sed 's/\[docfresh://;s/\]//'
115	}
116	
117	# Returns the "type" of the most prominent tag in the prompt, or "none"
118	# Priority order: wrapup > deepreview > review > docfresh > init > phase > none
119	classify_prompt_tags() {
120	    local prompt="$1"
121	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP"; then echo "wrapup"; return; fi
122	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi
123	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW"; then echo "review"; return; fi
124	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH"; then echo "docfresh"; return; fi
125	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT"; then echo "init"; return; fi
126	    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE"; then echo "phase"; return; fi
127	    echo "none"
128	}
129	
130	# ============================================================================
131	# WORK-UNIT LOG MANAGEMENT
132	# ============================================================================
133	
134	# Returns the path to the current active work-unit log, creating a new one if
135	# none exists or if the last one is stale (idle rotation).
136	get_current_log_path() {
137	    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"
138	
139	    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
140	        local current_log
141	        current_log=$(cat "$state_file")
142	        # Check if the file exists and isn't stale
143	        if [ -f "$current_log" ] && [ "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" -gt 0 ]; then
144	            local now last_mod
145	            now=$(date +%s)
146	            last_mod=$(stat -c %Y "$current_log" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
147	            local idle=$((now - last_mod))
148	            if [ "$idle" -lt "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" ]; then
149	                echo "$current_log"
150	                return
151	            fi
152	        elif [ -f "$current_log" ]; then
153	            echo "$current_log"
154	            return
155	        fi
156	    fi
157	
158	    # No valid current log; create a new one
159	    rotate_log_to_new "general"
160	}
161	
162	# Rotates to a new work-unit log file. Returns the new path.
163	# Optional first argument is a descriptor (slug) for the new log filename.
164	rotate_log_to_new() {
165	    local descriptor="${1:-general}"
166	    local date_str
167	    date_str=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
168	    local timestamp
169	    timestamp=$(date +%H%M%S)
170	    local new_log="$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR/${date_str}_${descriptor}_${timestamp}.md"
171	
172	    # Create the file with a header
173	    cat > "$new_log" <<EOF
174	# Work Unit Log: $descriptor
175	
176	Started: $(date -Iseconds)
177	Session: ${WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID:-unknown}
178	
179	---
180	
181	EOF
182	
183	    # Update state
184	    echo "$new_log" > "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"
185	
186	    hook_debug "Rotated to new log: $new_log"
187	    echo "$new_log"
188	}
189	
190	# ============================================================================
191	# TURN STATE MANAGEMENT
192	# ============================================================================
193	
194	# Records turn state for the Stop hook to consume.
195	# Called from UserPromptSubmit hook.
196	record_turn_state() {
197	    local session_id="$1"
198	    local prompt="$2"
199	    local transcript_path="$3"
200	
201	    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
202	
203	    # Get current transcript size so Stop hook knows where to start reading
204	    local transcript_offset=0
205	    if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
206	        transcript_offset=$(stat -c %s "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
207	    fi
208	
209	    local tag_type
210	    tag_type=$(classify_prompt_tags "$prompt")
211	
212	    local current_log
213	    current_log=$(get_current_log_path)
214	
215	    # Write state as JSON
216	    jq -nc \
217	        --arg sid "$session_id" \
218	        --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
219	        --arg prompt "$prompt" \
220	        --arg tag "$tag_type" \
221	        --arg log "$current_log" \
222	        --arg tpath "$transcript_path" \
223	        --argjson offset "$transcript_offset" \
224	        '{
225	            session_id: $sid,
226	            timestamp: $ts,
227	            prompt: $prompt,
228	            tag_type: $tag,
229	            log_path: $log,
230	            transcript_path: $tpath,
231	            transcript_offset: $offset
232	        }' > "$state_file"
233	
234	    hook_debug "Recorded turn state: tag=$tag_type log=$current_log"
235	}
236	
237	# Reads turn state file. Returns the JSON contents on stdout, or exits 1.
238	read_turn_state() {
239	    local session_id="$1"
240	    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
241	    if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
242	        return 1
243	    fi
244	    cat "$state_file"
245	}
246	
247	# Cleans up turn state after Stop hook processes it.
248	clear_turn_state() {
249	    local session_id="$1"
250	    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
251	    rm -f "$state_file"
252	}
253	
254	# ============================================================================
255	# CLAUDE -P INVOCATION HELPERS
256	# ============================================================================
257	
258	# Runs claude -p with a timeout and bubbles up a clear error if it times out.
259	# This is critical per the user's requirement that timeouts must be visible,
260	# not silent failures.
261	#
262	# Usage: claude_p_with_timeout "prompt text" [extra-claude-args...]
263	# Writes claude's output to stdout; returns its exit code.
264	claude_p_with_timeout() {
265	    local prompt="$1"
266	    shift
267	    local output
268	    local exit_code
269	
270	    # Use timeout(1) with explicit signal and exit code so we can detect it
271	    output=$(timeout --signal=TERM "$WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT" claude -p "$prompt" "$@" 2>&1)
272	    exit_code=$?
273	
274	    if [ $exit_code -eq 124 ]; then
275	        # GNU timeout returns 124 on timeout
276	        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p timed out after ${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT}s. Operation incomplete." >&2
277	        return 124
278	    elif [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
279	        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p failed with exit code $exit_code. Output: $output" >&2
280	        return $exit_code
281	    fi
282	
283	    echo "$output"
284	    return 0
285	}
286	
287	# ============================================================================
288	# UTILITY
289	# ============================================================================
290	
291	# Slug-ify a string for use in filenames
292	slugify() {
293	    echo "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed -E 's/[^a-z0-9]+/-/g' | sed -E 's/^-+|-+$//g'
294	}
295	
296	# Returns the SHA-truncated session ID for filenames (avoids absurdly long names)
297	short_session_id() {
298	    echo "$1" | head -c 8
299	}
300

#!/bin/bash
# lib-common.sh — shared helpers for the workflow suite
#
# Sourced by other scripts in this suite. Do not execute directly.
#
# This file is intended to be reviewed and customized by your local Claude
# Code instance for your specific project. Comments are deliberately verbose
# during the integration phase; strip them once you've adapted to your setup.

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# Project-local directories. All workflow state lives under .claude/
# These paths are relative to $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR which Claude Code sets.
export WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/logs"
export WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state"
export WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/summaries"
export WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/archive"
export WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/reviews"
export WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/docs/interim"

# Where the hook scripts live (used by claude -p invocations that need to
# reference helper scripts). Adjust if you put scripts elsewhere.
export WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts"

# Feature flags. Set to 0 to disable specific behaviors during wrap-up.
# Useful for cost control or while iterating on the suite.
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS:-1}"

# Timeout for claude -p invocations (seconds). Should be lower than the hook
# timeout so we surface our own timeout error instead of getting killed mid-run.
export WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT="${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT:-180}"

# Tag detection patterns. These are the supported tags in user prompts.
# Square brackets, lowercase. If you want to add new tags, add them here AND
# add corresponding handling in stop.sh.
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT='\[initprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE='\[phaseprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP='\[wrapup\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW='\[review\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW='\[deepreview\]'
# docfresh has a parameter: [docfresh:slug-name]
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH='\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]'

# Idle threshold in seconds for auto-rotating the work-unit log if no tag
# triggers a rotation. 4 hours by default. Set to 0 to disable idle rotation.
export WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS="${WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS:-14400}"

# ============================================================================
# DIRECTORY SETUP
# ============================================================================

ensure_workflow_dirs() {
    mkdir -p "$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"
}

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING & ERROR HANDLING
# ============================================================================

# Hook-internal logging. Writes to .claude/state/hook_debug.log
# Does NOT use stdout/stderr because those have specific meaning to Claude Code.
hook_debug() {
    local msg="$1"
    local hook_name="${HOOK_NAME:-unknown}"
    echo "$(date -Iseconds) [$hook_name] $msg" >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Emit a structured error that bubbles up to Claude so we don't silently fail.
# Per the user's requirement: when something fails, Claude needs to know.
# Use this for non-blocking errors (the hook ran but had a problem).
hook_error_to_claude() {
    local context="$1"
    local detail="$2"
    # additionalContext mechanism in hookSpecificOutput
    # The hook still exits 0 — we're using context injection, not blocking.
    jq -nc \
        --arg event "${CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT_NAME:-Unknown}" \
        --arg ctx "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR [$context]: $detail" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: $event, additionalContext: $ctx}}'
}

# Emit a fatal error and exit non-zero. Use sparingly — non-blocking events
# treat any non-zero exit as a non-blocking error message in the transcript,
# which is exactly what we want for visibility.
hook_fatal() {
    local msg="$1"
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: $msg" >&2
    exit 1
}

# ============================================================================
# TAG DETECTION
# ============================================================================

# Reads a prompt string, returns 0 if it contains the given tag pattern, 1 otherwise.
prompt_has_tag() {
    local prompt="$1"
    local tag_pattern="$2"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -qE "$tag_pattern"
}

# Extracts a docfresh slug from a prompt: [docfresh:my-slug] -> my-slug
extract_docfresh_slug() {
    local prompt="$1"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -oE '\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]' | head -1 | sed 's/\[docfresh://;s/\]//'
}

# Returns the "type" of the most prominent tag in the prompt, or "none"
# Priority order: wrapup > deepreview > review > docfresh > init > phase > none
classify_prompt_tags() {
    local prompt="$1"
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP"; then echo "wrapup"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW"; then echo "review"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH"; then echo "docfresh"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT"; then echo "init"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE"; then echo "phase"; return; fi
    echo "none"
}

# ============================================================================
# WORK-UNIT LOG MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

# Returns the path to the current active work-unit log, creating a new one if
# none exists or if the last one is stale (idle rotation).
get_current_log_path() {
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"

    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
        local current_log
        current_log=$(cat "$state_file")
        # Check if the file exists and isn't stale
        if [ -f "$current_log" ] && [ "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" -gt 0 ]; then
            local now last_mod
            now=$(date +%s)
            last_mod=$(stat -c %Y "$current_log" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            local idle=$((now - last_mod))
            if [ "$idle" -lt "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" ]; then
                echo "$current_log"
                return
            fi
        elif [ -f "$current_log" ]; then
            echo "$current_log"
            return
        fi
    fi

    # No valid current log; create a new one
    rotate_log_to_new "general"
}

# Rotates to a new work-unit log file. Returns the new path.
# Optional first argument is a descriptor (slug) for the new log filename.
rotate_log_to_new() {
    local descriptor="${1:-general}"
    local date_str
    date_str=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date +%H%M%S)
    local new_log="$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR/${date_str}_${descriptor}_${timestamp}.md"

    # Create the file with a header
    cat > "$new_log" <<EOF
# Work Unit Log: $descriptor

Started: $(date -Iseconds)
Session: ${WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID:-unknown}

---

EOF

    # Update state
    echo "$new_log" > "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"

    hook_debug "Rotated to new log: $new_log"
    echo "$new_log"
}

# ============================================================================
# TURN STATE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

# Records turn state for the Stop hook to consume.
# Called from UserPromptSubmit hook.
record_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local transcript_path="$3"

    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"

    # Get current transcript size so Stop hook knows where to start reading
    local transcript_offset=0
    if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        transcript_offset=$(stat -c %s "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    fi

    local tag_type
    tag_type=$(classify_prompt_tags "$prompt")

    local current_log
    current_log=$(get_current_log_path)

    # Write state as JSON
    jq -nc \
        --arg sid "$session_id" \
        --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
        --arg prompt "$prompt" \
        --arg tag "$tag_type" \
        --arg log "$current_log" \
        --arg tpath "$transcript_path" \
        --argjson offset "$transcript_offset" \
        '{
            session_id: $sid,
            timestamp: $ts,
            prompt: $prompt,
            tag_type: $tag,
            log_path: $log,
            transcript_path: $tpath,
            transcript_offset: $offset
        }' > "$state_file"

    hook_debug "Recorded turn state: tag=$tag_type log=$current_log"
}

# Reads turn state file. Returns the JSON contents on stdout, or exits 1.
read_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
    if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi
    cat "$state_file"
}

# Cleans up turn state after Stop hook processes it.
clear_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
    rm -f "$state_file"
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE -P INVOCATION HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Runs claude -p with a timeout and bubbles up a clear error if it times out.
# This is critical per the user's requirement that timeouts must be visible,
# not silent failures.
#
# Usage: claude_p_with_timeout "prompt text" [extra-claude-args...]
# Writes claude's output to stdout; returns its exit code.
claude_p_with_timeout() {
    local prompt="$1"
    shift
    local output
    local exit_code

    # Use timeout(1) with explicit signal and exit code so we can detect it
    output=$(timeout --signal=TERM "$WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT" claude -p "$prompt" "$@" 2>&1)
    exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -eq 124 ]; then
        # GNU timeout returns 124 on timeout
        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p timed out after ${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT}s. Operation incomplete." >&2
        return 124
    elif [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p failed with exit code $exit_code. Output: $output" >&2
        return $exit_code
    fi

    echo "$output"
    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITY
# ============================================================================

# Slug-ify a string for use in filenames
slugify() {
    echo "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed -E 's/[^a-z0-9]+/-/g' | sed -E 's/^-+|-+$//g'
}

# Returns the SHA-truncated session ID for filenames (avoids absurdly long names)
short_session_id() {
    echo "$1" | head -c 8
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# user-prompt-submit.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook
3	#
4	# Fires on every prompt submission. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Detect workflow tags in the prompt
6	#   2. Rotate the work-unit log if this is a new work unit ([initprompt])
7	#   3. Record turn state so the Stop hook knows what just happened
8	#   4. Optionally inject context for Claude about which tags it saw
9	#
10	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
11	#   - session_id, transcript_path, cwd, permission_mode, prompt
12	#
13	# Output:
14	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: prompt proceeds normally
15	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: prompt proceeds, context added
16	#   - We deliberately do NOT block prompts with this hook
17	
18	set -uo pipefail
19	
20	HOOK_NAME="user-prompt-submit"
21	export HOOK_NAME
22	
23	# Source the common library (path resolved via CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
24	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
25	if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
26	    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
27	    exit 1
28	fi
29	# shellcheck source=/dev/null
30	source "$LIB_PATH"
31	
32	# Read JSON input from stdin
33	INPUT=$(cat)
34	
35	# Extract fields
36	SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
37	TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
38	PROMPT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
39	
40	export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
41	
42	# Ensure directory structure exists (idempotent)
43	ensure_workflow_dirs
44	
45	hook_debug "session=$SESSION_ID prompt_length=${#PROMPT}"
46	
47	# Classify the prompt
48	TAG_TYPE=$(classify_prompt_tags "$PROMPT")
49	hook_debug "tag_type=$TAG_TYPE"
50	
51	# Rotate log if this is a new work unit
52	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
53	    init)
54	        # Extract a descriptor from the prompt (first 50 chars, slugified)
55	        # User can include something hint-y after [initprompt] to name the work unit
56	        DESCRIPTOR_TEXT=$(echo "$PROMPT" | sed -E 's/\[initprompt\]//' | head -c 80)
57	        DESCRIPTOR=$(slugify "$DESCRIPTOR_TEXT")
58	        if [ -z "$DESCRIPTOR" ]; then
59	            DESCRIPTOR="work-unit"
60	        fi
61	        # Cap the descriptor length to keep filenames sane
62	        DESCRIPTOR=$(echo "$DESCRIPTOR" | head -c 40)
63	        rotate_log_to_new "$DESCRIPTOR" > /dev/null
64	        hook_debug "rotated log on initprompt with descriptor=$DESCRIPTOR"
65	        ;;
66	    *)
67	        # No rotation for other tag types; current log continues
68	        # If no current log exists, get_current_log_path will create one
69	        get_current_log_path > /dev/null
70	        ;;
71	esac
72	
73	# Record turn state for the Stop hook
74	record_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" "$PROMPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
75	
76	# Optionally inject context for Claude about what's happening
77	# This is useful when a tag triggers special behavior, so Claude knows
78	case "$TAG_TYPE" in
79	    init)
80	        CONTEXT="A new work unit was started ([initprompt] detected). A fresh work-unit log was created. Proceed with the task as normal; the workflow infrastructure will capture the work."
81	        ;;
82	    wrapup)
83	        CONTEXT="A work-unit wrap-up was requested ([wrapup] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will automatically generate a summary, run a review of work done since the last [initprompt], and propose documentation updates. Proceed with any final steps for this work unit."
84	        ;;
85	    review)
86	        CONTEXT="A mid-work-unit review was requested ([review] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run a review of work done since the last [initprompt] and surface any concerns. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
87	        ;;
88	    deepreview)
89	        CONTEXT="A deep review was requested ([deepreview] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run an in-depth review including full file contents of changed code. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
90	        ;;
91	    docfresh)
92	        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$PROMPT")
93	        CONTEXT="A targeted documentation refresh was requested ([docfresh:$SLUG] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will update the interim doc for '$SLUG' based on recent work. Proceed with the task."
94	        ;;
95	    *)
96	        # No context injection for plain prompts or phase prompts
97	        exit 0
98	        ;;
99	esac
100	
101	# Emit context JSON
102	jq -nc \
103	    --arg ctx "$CONTEXT" \
104	    '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "UserPromptSubmit", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
105	
106	exit 0
107

#!/bin/bash
# user-prompt-submit.sh — UserPromptSubmit hook
#
# Fires on every prompt submission. Responsibilities:
#   1. Detect workflow tags in the prompt
#   2. Rotate the work-unit log if this is a new work unit ([initprompt])
#   3. Record turn state so the Stop hook knows what just happened
#   4. Optionally inject context for Claude about which tags it saw
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - session_id, transcript_path, cwd, permission_mode, prompt
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: prompt proceeds normally
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext: prompt proceeds, context added
#   - We deliberately do NOT block prompts with this hook

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="user-prompt-submit"
export HOOK_NAME

# Source the common library (path resolved via CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

# Read JSON input from stdin
INPUT=$(cat)

# Extract fields
SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
PROMPT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"

# Ensure directory structure exists (idempotent)
ensure_workflow_dirs

hook_debug "session=$SESSION_ID prompt_length=${#PROMPT}"

# Classify the prompt
TAG_TYPE=$(classify_prompt_tags "$PROMPT")
hook_debug "tag_type=$TAG_TYPE"

# Rotate log if this is a new work unit
case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    init)
        # Extract a descriptor from the prompt (first 50 chars, slugified)
        # User can include something hint-y after [initprompt] to name the work unit
        DESCRIPTOR_TEXT=$(echo "$PROMPT" | sed -E 's/\[initprompt\]//' | head -c 80)
        DESCRIPTOR=$(slugify "$DESCRIPTOR_TEXT")
        if [ -z "$DESCRIPTOR" ]; then
            DESCRIPTOR="work-unit"
        fi
        # Cap the descriptor length to keep filenames sane
        DESCRIPTOR=$(echo "$DESCRIPTOR" | head -c 40)
        rotate_log_to_new "$DESCRIPTOR" > /dev/null
        hook_debug "rotated log on initprompt with descriptor=$DESCRIPTOR"
        ;;
    *)
        # No rotation for other tag types; current log continues
        # If no current log exists, get_current_log_path will create one
        get_current_log_path > /dev/null
        ;;
esac

# Record turn state for the Stop hook
record_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" "$PROMPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"

# Optionally inject context for Claude about what's happening
# This is useful when a tag triggers special behavior, so Claude knows
case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    init)
        CONTEXT="A new work unit was started ([initprompt] detected). A fresh work-unit log was created. Proceed with the task as normal; the workflow infrastructure will capture the work."
        ;;
    wrapup)
        CONTEXT="A work-unit wrap-up was requested ([wrapup] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will automatically generate a summary, run a review of work done since the last [initprompt], and propose documentation updates. Proceed with any final steps for this work unit."
        ;;
    review)
        CONTEXT="A mid-work-unit review was requested ([review] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run a review of work done since the last [initprompt] and surface any concerns. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
        ;;
    deepreview)
        CONTEXT="A deep review was requested ([deepreview] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will run an in-depth review including full file contents of changed code. Proceed with the task; the review runs separately."
        ;;
    docfresh)
        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$PROMPT")
        CONTEXT="A targeted documentation refresh was requested ([docfresh:$SLUG] detected). After this turn completes, the workflow infrastructure will update the interim doc for '$SLUG' based on recent work. Proceed with the task."
        ;;
    *)
        # No context injection for plain prompts or phase prompts
        exit 0
        ;;
esac

# Emit context JSON
jq -nc \
    --arg ctx "$CONTEXT" \
    '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "UserPromptSubmit", additionalContext: $ctx}}'

exit 0

40	        ;;

43	        ;;
44	    MultiEdit)
45	        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
46	        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
47	        ;;

51	        ;;

90	
91	    # OpenAI

93	    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'
94	
95	    # Anthropic

97	
98	    # Stripe
99	    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
100	    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'
101	
102	    # Google
103	    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'
104	
105	    # Slack

116	    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'

126	fi

130	MATCHED_DESC=""

#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-edit.sh — PreToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
#
# Fires before file writes. Responsibilities:
#   1. Scan the content being written for common credential/secret patterns
#   2. Block the write if matches are found, with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, content, new_string, ...}
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: write proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": write blocked
#
# Note: This is a moderate-aggressiveness scanner. Patterns chosen for low
# false-positive rate. If you find this catching too many false positives,
# adjust SECRET_PATTERNS below. If you find it missing real secrets, ditto.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-edit"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')

# Extract content based on which tool fired
CONTENT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""')
        ;;
    Edit)
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // ""')
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        # MultiEdit has an edits array, each with new_string
        CONTENT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '[.tool_input.edits[]?.new_string // ""] | join("\n")')
        ;;
    *)
        # Unknown tool variant; allow through
        exit 0
        ;;
esac

# Skip empty content
if [ -z "$CONTENT" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# Skip if the file path looks like a test fixture, documentation, or
# explicitly-marked sample. Reduces false positives for example credentials
# in docs.
case "$FILE_PATH" in
    *test*|*tests*|*spec*|*fixture*|*example*|*sample*|*.md|*.rst|*.txt)
        # These get a lighter scan — only check for very specific real-key prefixes
        SCAN_MODE="light"
        ;;
    *)
        SCAN_MODE="full"
        ;;
esac

# ============================================================================
# SECRET PATTERNS
# ============================================================================
# Each pattern below is a regex matched case-insensitively against content.
# Patterns are designed to catch real-world credentials with low false positives.
#
# To add a new pattern: append a "pattern|description" line to SECRET_PATTERNS.
# To remove a pattern: delete its line.
# To tune sensitivity: edit the regexes themselves (more specific = fewer FPs).

SECRET_PATTERNS=(
    # AWS keys
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'aws_secret_access_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}|AWS Secret Key'

    # GitHub tokens
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}|GitHub fine-grained PAT'

    # OpenAI
    'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{20}T3BlbkFJ[A-Za-z0-9]{20}|OpenAI API key (legacy)'
    'sk-proj-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{40,}|OpenAI project key'

    # Anthropic
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'

    # Stripe
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    'rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live restricted key'

    # Google
    'AIza[0-9A-Za-z_-]{35}|Google API key'

    # Slack
    'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}|Slack token'

    # Generic high-entropy patterns near credential identifiers
    # Only used in "full" scan mode to reduce false positives in docs/tests
    'private_key\s*[:=]\s*["'"'"']?-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

# Light mode patterns: only obvious real keys, skip identifier-based heuristics
LIGHT_PATTERNS=(
    'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}|AWS Access Key ID'
    'gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9]{36,255}|GitHub token'
    'sk-ant-api[0-9]{2}-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{80,}|Anthropic API key'
    'sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{24,}|Stripe live secret key'
    '-----BEGIN[ A-Z]+PRIVATE KEY-----|Private key block'
)

if [ "$SCAN_MODE" = "light" ]; then
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${LIGHT_PATTERNS[@]}")
else
    PATTERNS_TO_USE=("${SECRET_PATTERNS[@]}")
fi

# Scan
MATCHED_PATTERN=""
MATCHED_DESC=""
for entry in "${PATTERNS_TO_USE[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        MATCHED_PATTERN="$pattern"
        MATCHED_DESC="$desc"
        break
    fi
done

if [ -n "$MATCHED_PATTERN" ]; then
    # Block the write
    REASON="Secret scanner blocked this write. Detected pattern matching: $MATCHED_DESC. If this is a false positive (example credential, test fixture, etc.), move the file to a path containing 'test', 'example', 'fixture', or 'sample', or modify the secret scanner in .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-edit.sh to exclude this pattern."

    if [ -n "${HOOK_NAME+x}" ] && command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        hook_debug "BLOCKED: $FILE_PATH matched pattern: $MATCHED_DESC"
    fi

    jq -nc \
        --arg reason "$REASON" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
    exit 0
fi

# No secrets detected; allow
exit 0

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# pre-tool-use-bash.sh — PreToolUse hook for Bash
3	#
4	# Fires before Bash commands execute. Responsibilities:
5	#   1. Detect catastrophically dangerous commands (rm -rf root, dd to disk, etc.)
6	#   2. Block them with a clear reason
7	#
8	# Input (from stdin, JSON):
9	#   - tool_name: "Bash"
10	#   - tool_input.command: the command string
11	#
12	# Output:
13	#   - exit 0 with no JSON: command proceeds
14	#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": blocked
15	#
16	# Philosophy: this hook only blocks things that are almost certainly destructive
17	# mistakes. Normal cleanup commands (rm of build artifacts, etc.) are allowed.
18	# If you find yourself fighting this hook, expand the allowlist patterns below.
19	
20	set -uo pipefail
21	
22	HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-bash"
23	export HOOK_NAME
24	
25	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
26	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
27	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
28	    source "$LIB_PATH"
29	fi
30	
31	INPUT=$(cat)
32	COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')
33	
34	if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
35	    exit 0
36	fi
37	
38	# ============================================================================
39	# DANGEROUS PATTERNS
40	# ============================================================================
41	# Each entry is "pattern|description". Patterns are bash-extended regex,
42	# matched case-insensitively against the command.
43	
44	DANGEROUS_PATTERNS=(
45	    # rm -rf on root or home
46	    'rm[[:space:]]+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-rf|-fr)[[:space:]]+(/$|/[[:space:]]|/\*|\$HOME|~/?$|/home/?$|/root/?$)|rm -rf on root or home directory'
47	
48	    # dd to a block device
49	    'dd[[:space:]].*of=/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|dd to a block device'
50	
51	    # mkfs on a block device
52	    'mkfs.*/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|mkfs on a block device'
53	
54	    # Fork bomb
55	    ':\(\)\{[[:space:]]*:\|:&[[:space:]]*\}|Fork bomb pattern'
56	
57	    # chmod 000 on critical paths
58	    'chmod[[:space:]]+(0?)?000[[:space:]]+(/$|/etc|/bin|/usr|/var)|chmod 000 on system directory'
59	
60	    # Pipe curl/wget to shell
61	    'curl[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping curl to shell'
62	    'wget[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping wget to shell'
63	
64	    # Force-push to main/master
65	    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+.*--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
66	    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
67	
68	    # Disable firewall
69	    'ufw[[:space:]]+disable|Disabling firewall (ufw)'
70	    'iptables[[:space:]]+-F|Flushing iptables rules'
71	
72	    # Modify root authorized_keys
73	    '(echo|cat).*>>?[[:space:]]*/root/\.ssh/authorized_keys|Modifying root authorized_keys'
74	)
75	
76	for entry in "${DANGEROUS_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
77	    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
78	    desc="${entry##*|}"
79	    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
80	        REASON="Safety check blocked this command. Detected: $desc. Command was: $COMMAND. If this is intentional, run it manually outside of Claude Code, or modify .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.sh to remove this pattern."
81	
82	        if command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
83	            hook_debug "BLOCKED: $desc — command: $COMMAND"
84	        fi
85	
86	        jq -nc \
87	            --arg reason "$REASON" \
88	            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
89	        exit 0
90	    fi
91	done
92	
93	# Command is fine; allow
94	exit 0
95

#!/bin/bash
# pre-tool-use-bash.sh — PreToolUse hook for Bash
#
# Fires before Bash commands execute. Responsibilities:
#   1. Detect catastrophically dangerous commands (rm -rf root, dd to disk, etc.)
#   2. Block them with a clear reason
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name: "Bash"
#   - tool_input.command: the command string
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: command proceeds
#   - exit 0 with hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecision: "deny": blocked
#
# Philosophy: this hook only blocks things that are almost certainly destructive
# mistakes. Normal cleanup commands (rm of build artifacts, etc.) are allowed.
# If you find yourself fighting this hook, expand the allowlist patterns below.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="pre-tool-use-bash"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""')

if [ -z "$COMMAND" ]; then
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# DANGEROUS PATTERNS
# ============================================================================
# Each entry is "pattern|description". Patterns are bash-extended regex,
# matched case-insensitively against the command.

DANGEROUS_PATTERNS=(
    # rm -rf on root or home
    'rm[[:space:]]+(-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|-[a-zA-Z]*f[a-zA-Z]*r|-rf|-fr)[[:space:]]+(/$|/[[:space:]]|/\*|\$HOME|~/?$|/home/?$|/root/?$)|rm -rf on root or home directory'

    # dd to a block device
    'dd[[:space:]].*of=/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|dd to a block device'

    # mkfs on a block device
    'mkfs.*/dev/(sd|nvme|hd|vd)|mkfs on a block device'

    # Fork bomb
    ':\(\)\{[[:space:]]*:\|:&[[:space:]]*\}|Fork bomb pattern'

    # chmod 000 on critical paths
    'chmod[[:space:]]+(0?)?000[[:space:]]+(/$|/etc|/bin|/usr|/var)|chmod 000 on system directory'

    # Pipe curl/wget to shell
    'curl[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping curl to shell'
    'wget[[:space:]].*\|[[:space:]]*(sh|bash|zsh)([[:space:]]|$)|Piping wget to shell'

    # Force-push to main/master
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+.*--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'
    'git[[:space:]]+push[[:space:]]+--force.*[[:space:]](main|master)([[:space:]]|$)|Force-push to main/master branch'

    # Disable firewall
    'ufw[[:space:]]+disable|Disabling firewall (ufw)'
    'iptables[[:space:]]+-F|Flushing iptables rules'

    # Modify root authorized_keys
    '(echo|cat).*>>?[[:space:]]*/root/\.ssh/authorized_keys|Modifying root authorized_keys'
)

for entry in "${DANGEROUS_PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    pattern="${entry%%|*}"
    desc="${entry##*|}"
    if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
        REASON="Safety check blocked this command. Detected: $desc. Command was: $COMMAND. If this is intentional, run it manually outside of Claude Code, or modify .claude/hooks/pre-tool-use-bash.sh to remove this pattern."

        if command -v hook_debug >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            hook_debug "BLOCKED: $desc — command: $COMMAND"
        fi

        jq -nc \
            --arg reason "$REASON" \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "PreToolUse", permissionDecision: "deny", permissionDecisionReason: $reason}}'
        exit 0
    fi
done

# Command is fine; allow
exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# post-tool-use.sh — PostToolUse hook for Edit/Write/MultiEdit
#
# Fires after file writes succeed. Responsibilities:
#   1. Append a structural-tagged entry to the current work-unit log
#      indicating which file was modified.
#
# This is the cheap, mechanical part of the verification system. The expensive
# LLM-based summary/review happens in the Stop hook, and only on [wrapup].
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - tool_name, tool_input.{file_path, ...}, tool_response
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 always; no decision control needed for PostToolUse

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="post-tool-use"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0  # Fail silently rather than disrupt the user's session
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_name // ""')
FILE_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
ensure_workflow_dirs

# Determine the current work-unit log
LOG_PATH=$(get_current_log_path)

# Determine the kind of change
TAG="#FILE"
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Write) TAG="#WRITE" ;;
    Edit) TAG="#EDIT" ;;
    MultiEdit) TAG="#MULTIEDIT" ;;
esac

# For Edit/MultiEdit, we can also note approximate change size by looking at
# the old_string/new_string lengths.
SIZE_HINT=""
case "$TOOL_NAME" in
    Edit)
        OLD_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.old_string // "" | length')
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.new_string // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${OLD_LEN}→${NEW_LEN} chars)"
        ;;
    Write)
        NEW_LEN=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.content // "" | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${NEW_LEN} chars written)"
        ;;
    MultiEdit)
        EDIT_COUNT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.edits // [] | length')
        SIZE_HINT=" (${EDIT_COUNT} edits)"
        ;;
esac

# Append to log
{
    echo ""
    echo "## $TAG \`$FILE_PATH\`$SIZE_HINT"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

hook_debug "Logged $TAG for $FILE_PATH"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# stop.sh — Stop hook
#
# Fires when Claude finishes responding to a turn. Responsibilities:
#   1. Extract the turn's content from the transcript into the work-unit log
#   2. If the prompt for this turn had [wrapup], trigger wrap-up sequence
#   3. If [review], trigger review only
#   4. If [deepreview], trigger deepreview
#   5. If [docfresh:slug], trigger targeted doc refresh
#   6. Clean up turn state
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - session_id, transcript_path, hook_event_name, stop_hook_active
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 with no JSON: Claude finishes normally
#   - We deliberately do NOT block Claude from finishing (would loop)
#
# CRITICAL: Per the Claude Code docs, returning decision:"block" from a Stop
# hook PREVENTS Claude from finishing and forces another turn. That's the
# opposite of what we want. We always exit 0 cleanly.

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="stop"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR: lib-common.sh not found at $LIB_PATH" >&2
    exit 0  # Don't block Claude
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.stop_hook_active // false')

export WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID="$SESSION_ID"
ensure_workflow_dirs

# If stop_hook_active is true, we've already been called for this stop event.
# Avoid infinite loops.
if [ "$STOP_HOOK_ACTIVE" = "true" ]; then
    hook_debug "stop_hook_active=true, skipping to avoid loop"
    exit 0
fi

# Read turn state recorded by UserPromptSubmit
STATE=$(read_turn_state "$SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null) || {
    hook_debug "no turn state found for session $SESSION_ID; skipping extraction"
    exit 0
}

TAG_TYPE=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.tag_type // "none"')
LOG_PATH=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.log_path // ""')
ORIG_PROMPT=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.prompt // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET=$(echo "$STATE" | jq -r '.transcript_offset // 0')

hook_debug "Stop hook: tag_type=$TAG_TYPE log=$LOG_PATH"

# ============================================================================
# STEP 1: Extract turn from transcript into log
# ============================================================================

if [ -z "$LOG_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    hook_debug "Cannot extract: log=$LOG_PATH transcript=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH"
else
    # Use the extract-turn.sh script
    EXTRACT_SCRIPT="${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/extract-turn.sh"
    if [ -x "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" ]; then
        "$EXTRACT_SCRIPT" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$TRANSCRIPT_OFFSET" "$LOG_PATH" "$ORIG_PROMPT" 2>>"$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" || {
            hook_debug "extract-turn.sh failed (exit $?)"
        }
    else
        hook_debug "extract-turn.sh not found or not executable at $EXTRACT_SCRIPT"
    fi
fi

# ============================================================================
# STEP 2: Tag-driven follow-up actions
# ============================================================================

# All the LLM-invoking actions run in the background (async) so they don't
# block Claude Code from finishing the turn. They write their results to
# files; the user can view them when ready.

case "$TAG_TYPE" in
    wrapup)
        hook_debug "Triggering wrap-up sequence"
        # Run all three: summary, review, doc update
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/generate-summary.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
            fi
            if [ "$WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS" = "1" ]; then
                "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID"
            fi
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/wrapup.log" 2>&1 &
        BG_PID=$!
        hook_debug "wrap-up sequence started in background, pid=$BG_PID"

        # Inject context for Claude letting it know wrap-up is running
        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Work-unit wrap-up sequence started in background. Summary, review, and doc updates are being produced asynchronously. Results will appear in .claude/summaries/, .claude/reviews/, and .claude/docs/interim/ when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    review)
        hook_debug "Triggering shallow review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "shallow"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        BG_PID=$!
        hook_debug "review started in background, pid=$BG_PID"

        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Shallow review started in background. Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    deepreview)
        hook_debug "Triggering deep review"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/run-review.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "deep"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/review.log" 2>&1 &
        BG_PID=$!
        hook_debug "deep review started in background, pid=$BG_PID"

        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Deep review started in background (will take longer than shallow review). Check .claude/reviews/ for the report when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    docfresh)
        SLUG=$(extract_docfresh_slug "$ORIG_PROMPT")
        hook_debug "Triggering docfresh for slug=$SLUG"
        (
            "${WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR}/update-docs.sh" "$LOG_PATH" "$SESSION_ID" "$SLUG"
        ) >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/docfresh.log" 2>&1 &
        BG_PID=$!
        hook_debug "docfresh started in background, pid=$BG_PID"

        jq -nc \
            --arg ctx "Documentation refresh for '$SLUG' started in background. Check .claude/docs/interim/$SLUG.md for the update when complete." \
            '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: "Stop", additionalContext: $ctx}}'
        ;;

    *)
        # No follow-up actions for plain prompts, initprompt, or phaseprompt
        ;;
esac

# ============================================================================
# STEP 3: Clean up turn state
# ============================================================================

clear_turn_state "$SESSION_ID"

exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# session-end.sh — SessionEnd hook
#
# Fires when a Claude Code session terminates. Responsibilities:
#   1. Archive the full transcript JSONL to .claude/archive/
#      (Claude Code's own transcripts get cleaned up after 30 days by default;
#       archiving preserves them indefinitely)
#
# Input (from stdin, JSON):
#   - session_id, transcript_path, reason
#
# Output:
#   - exit 0 always; SessionEnd cannot block

set -uo pipefail

HOOK_NAME="session-end"
export HOOK_NAME

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ ! -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    exit 0
fi
# shellcheck source=/dev/null
source "$LIB_PATH"

INPUT=$(cat)

SESSION_ID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.session_id // ""')
TRANSCRIPT_PATH=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.transcript_path // ""')
REASON=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.reason // "unknown"')

ensure_workflow_dirs

if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ] || [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    hook_debug "No transcript to archive (path=$TRANSCRIPT_PATH)"
    exit 0
fi

# Archive with a name that includes session ID and timestamp
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)
SHORT_SID=$(short_session_id "$SESSION_ID")
ARCHIVE_PATH="${WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR}/session_${TIMESTAMP}_${SHORT_SID}_${REASON}.jsonl"

if cp "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" "$ARCHIVE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then
    hook_debug "Archived transcript to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
else
    hook_debug "Failed to archive transcript from $TRANSCRIPT_PATH to $ARCHIVE_PATH"
fi

# Optionally clean up old turn state files for this session (none should remain
# if Stop hook ran, but defensive)
rm -f "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${SESSION_ID}.json"

exit 0
