1	#!/bin/bash
2	# update-docs.sh — Update interim documentation based on work unit changes
3	#
4	# Usage:
5	#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID           # auto-detect affected docs
6	#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID SLUG      # update specific doc by slug
7	#
8	# The auto-detect mode:
9	#   1. Identifies files modified in this work unit
10	#   2. Finds existing interim docs whose `implementation:` field references
11	#      any of those files
12	#   3. Updates those docs to reflect the new behavior
13	#   4. If no existing doc covers a modified file, creates a new interim doc
14	#
15	# The targeted mode (with SLUG):
16	#   1. Reads the existing doc at .claude/docs/interim/SLUG.md
17	#   2. Updates it based on recent work unit content
18	
19	set -uo pipefail
20	
21	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
22	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
23	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
24	    source "$LIB_PATH"
25	fi
26	
27	LOG_PATH="$1"
28	SESSION_ID="$2"
29	TARGET_SLUG="${3:-}"
30	
31	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
32	    echo "update-docs: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	ensure_workflow_dirs
37	
38	LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
39	LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
40	TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
41	
42	# Try to find the corresponding summary
43	SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
44	SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
45	if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
46	    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
47	fi
48	
49	# ============================================================================
50	# Identify modified files
51	# ============================================================================
52	
53	FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)
54	
55	if [ -z "$FILES_MODIFIED" ] && [ -z "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
56	    echo "update-docs: no files modified and no target slug; nothing to do"
57	    exit 0
58	fi
59	
60	# ============================================================================
61	# Identify affected docs
62	# ============================================================================
63	
64	AFFECTED_DOCS=()
65	
66	if [ -n "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
67	    TARGET_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${TARGET_SLUG}.md"
68	    if [ -f "$TARGET_DOC" ]; then
69	        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
70	    else
71	        # Doc doesn't exist; we'll create it below
72	        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
73	    fi
74	else
75	    # Auto-detect: scan existing interim docs for implementation: entries matching files
76	    if [ -d "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR" ]; then
77	        while IFS= read -r f; do
78	            if grep -qF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null; then
79	                while IFS= read -r doc; do
80	                    # Check if this doc actually references this file in its implementation: section
81	                    if grep -A 20 'implementation:' "$doc" 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "$f"; then
82	                        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$doc")
83	                    fi
84	                done < <(grep -lF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null)
85	            fi
86	        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"
87	    fi
88	
89	    # Deduplicate
90	    if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
91	        readarray -t AFFECTED_DOCS < <(printf '%s\n' "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}" | sort -u)
92	    fi
93	fi
94	
95	# ============================================================================
96	# Update each affected doc
97	# ============================================================================
98	
99	if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
100	    # No existing docs cover the modified files. Create a new interim doc.
101	    # We'll let claude -p figure out an appropriate slug from the work unit.
102	
103	    NEW_SLUG_PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
104	Based on this work unit summary and log, suggest a short kebab-case slug
105	(3-6 words, lowercase, hyphen-separated) for an interim documentation file
106	covering the feature or area this work unit touched.
107	
108	Output ONLY the slug, nothing else. No quotes, no explanation.
109	
110	Summary:
111	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
112	
113	Log:
114	$LOG_CONTENT
115	EOF
116	)
117	
118	    NEW_SLUG=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$NEW_SLUG_PROMPT" 2>&1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 60)
119	    if [ -z "$NEW_SLUG" ] || ! echo "$NEW_SLUG" | grep -qE '^[a-z0-9-]+$'; then
120	        # Fallback to a generic slug based on the log filename
121	        NEW_SLUG=$(slugify "$LOG_FILENAME" | head -c 50)
122	    fi
123	
124	    NEW_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${NEW_SLUG}.md"
125	    AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$NEW_DOC")
126	fi
127	
128	for doc_path in "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}"; do
129	    SLUG=$(basename "$doc_path" .md)
130	
131	    if [ -f "$doc_path" ]; then
132	        EXISTING_DOC=$(cat "$doc_path")
133	        MODE="update"
134	    else
135	        EXISTING_DOC=""
136	        MODE="create"
137	    fi
138	
139	    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
140	You are maintaining an interim design document for a Claude Code project.
141	
142	Mode: $MODE
143	Document slug: $SLUG
144	Document path: $doc_path
145	
146	This is an INTERIM doc (status: interim) under .claude/docs/interim/. It will
147	eventually be merged into a canonical structure. For now, keep it focused on
148	describing CURRENT REALITY of the code.
149	
150	Schema for interim docs (frontmatter at top of file, in YAML between --- lines):
151	
152	  id: $SLUG
153	  title: <human-readable title>
154	  status: interim
155	  created: <YYYY-MM-DD, set once on creation>
156	  last_updated: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date when updating>
157	  last_synced_with_code: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date>
158	  work_units:
159	    - <list of work-unit log filenames that contributed>
160	  implementation:
161	    - <list of code file paths this doc describes>
162	  related:
163	    - <informal list of related doc slugs>
164	
165	Body sections (in this order):
166	  # <Title>
167	  <1-3 sentence summary>
168	
169	  ## Purpose
170	  <what this feature/area does>
171	
172	  ## Behavior
173	  <how it behaves, including caveats>
174	
175	  ## Implementation Notes
176	  <where the code lives, key design decisions>
177	
178	  ## Changelog
179	  <!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
180	  ### YYYY-MM-DD
181	  <Description of changes made on this date. Most recent on top.>
182	
183	YOUR JOB:
184	$(if [ "$MODE" = "create" ]; then
185	echo "- This doc does NOT yet exist. Create it fresh based on the work unit log and summary."
186	echo "- Set 'created' and 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' all to: $TODAY"
187	echo "- Populate work_units with: $LOG_FILENAME"
188	echo "- Populate implementation with the file paths actually modified in this work unit"
189	echo "- Add an initial changelog entry dated $TODAY describing the creation"
190	else
191	echo "- This doc EXISTS. Update it to reflect new behavior from this work unit."
192	echo "- Preserve the existing 'created' date (do NOT change it)"
193	echo "- Update 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' to: $TODAY"
194	echo "- APPEND the current work-unit log filename to work_units (if not already there): $LOG_FILENAME"
195	echo "- UPDATE implementation list if new files were touched"
196	echo "- UPDATE body sections to reflect new behavior (overwrite, don't append, in body sections)"
197	echo "- ADD a new changelog entry at the TOP of the Changelog section dated $TODAY, describing what changed"
198	echo "- KEEP previous changelog entries in place beneath the new one"
199	fi)
200	
201	OUTPUT RULES:
202	- Output ONLY the complete file content (frontmatter + body), nothing else
203	- Start with the opening --- of frontmatter
204	- Do NOT include preamble, explanation, or markdown code fences around the output
205	- The output replaces the entire file contents
206	
207	WORK UNIT LOG:
208	---
209	$LOG_CONTENT
210	---
211	
212	$([ -n "$SUMMARY_CONTENT" ] && echo "WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
213	---
214	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
215	---
216	")
217	
218	$([ "$MODE" = "update" ] && echo "EXISTING DOC CONTENT:
219	---
220	$EXISTING_DOC
221	---
222	")
223	
224	Produce the complete file content now.
225	EOF
226	)
227	
228	    NEW_CONTENT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
229	    EXIT_CODE=$?
230	
231	    if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
232	        echo "update-docs: failed to update $doc_path (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
233	        # Write a proposal file so the update isn't lost
234	        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
235	        {
236	            echo "# Doc Update Failed"
237	            echo ""
238	            echo "Generated: $(date -Iseconds)"
239	            echo "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
240	            echo ""
241	            echo "Output:"
242	            echo "\`\`\`"
243	            echo "$NEW_CONTENT"
244	            echo "\`\`\`"
245	        } > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
246	        continue
247	    fi
248	
249	    # Strip any wrapping markdown code fences claude might have added despite our instructions
250	    NEW_CONTENT=$(echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | sed -E '1s/^```(markdown|md)?$//' | sed -E '$s/^```$//')
251	
252	    # Sanity check: must start with frontmatter
253	    if ! echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | head -1 | grep -q '^---'; then
254	        echo "update-docs: invalid output for $doc_path (no frontmatter); writing as proposal" >&2
255	        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
256	        echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
257	        continue
258	    fi
259	
260	    # Auto-apply per the user's preference
261	    echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$doc_path"
262	    echo "update-docs: wrote $doc_path ($MODE)"
263	done
264	
265	exit 0
266

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# update-docs.sh — Update interim documentation based on work unit changes
#
# Usage:
#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID           # auto-detect affected docs
#   update-docs.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID SLUG      # update specific doc by slug
#
# The auto-detect mode:
#   1. Identifies files modified in this work unit
#   2. Finds existing interim docs whose `implementation:` field references
#      any of those files
#   3. Updates those docs to reflect the new behavior
#   4. If no existing doc covers a modified file, creates a new interim doc
#
# The targeted mode (with SLUG):
#   1. Reads the existing doc at .claude/docs/interim/SLUG.md
#   2. Updates it based on recent work unit content

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

LOG_PATH="$1"
SESSION_ID="$2"
TARGET_SLUG="${3:-}"

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "update-docs: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_workflow_dirs

LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
TODAY=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)

# Try to find the corresponding summary
SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
fi

# ============================================================================
# Identify modified files
# ============================================================================

FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)

if [ -z "$FILES_MODIFIED" ] && [ -z "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
    echo "update-docs: no files modified and no target slug; nothing to do"
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# Identify affected docs
# ============================================================================

AFFECTED_DOCS=()

if [ -n "$TARGET_SLUG" ]; then
    TARGET_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${TARGET_SLUG}.md"
    if [ -f "$TARGET_DOC" ]; then
        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
    else
        # Doc doesn't exist; we'll create it below
        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$TARGET_DOC")
    fi
else
    # Auto-detect: scan existing interim docs for implementation: entries matching files
    if [ -d "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r f; do
            if grep -qF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null; then
                while IFS= read -r doc; do
                    # Check if this doc actually references this file in its implementation: section
                    if grep -A 20 'implementation:' "$doc" 2>/dev/null | grep -qF -- "$f"; then
                        AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$doc")
                    fi
                done < <(grep -lF -- "$f" "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"/*.md 2>/dev/null)
            fi
        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"
    fi

    # Deduplicate
    if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
        readarray -t AFFECTED_DOCS < <(printf '%s\n' "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}" | sort -u)
    fi
fi

# ============================================================================
# Update each affected doc
# ============================================================================

if [ ${#AFFECTED_DOCS[@]} -eq 0 ]; then
    # No existing docs cover the modified files. Create a new interim doc.
    # We'll let claude -p figure out an appropriate slug from the work unit.

    NEW_SLUG_PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
Based on this work unit summary and log, suggest a short kebab-case slug
(3-6 words, lowercase, hyphen-separated) for an interim documentation file
covering the feature or area this work unit touched.

Output ONLY the slug, nothing else. No quotes, no explanation.

Summary:
$SUMMARY_CONTENT

Log:
$LOG_CONTENT
EOF
)

    NEW_SLUG=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$NEW_SLUG_PROMPT" 2>&1 | tr -d ' \n' | head -c 60)
    if [ -z "$NEW_SLUG" ] || ! echo "$NEW_SLUG" | grep -qE '^[a-z0-9-]+$'; then
        # Fallback to a generic slug based on the log filename
        NEW_SLUG=$(slugify "$LOG_FILENAME" | head -c 50)
    fi

    NEW_DOC="${WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR}/${NEW_SLUG}.md"
    AFFECTED_DOCS+=("$NEW_DOC")
fi

for doc_path in "${AFFECTED_DOCS[@]}"; do
    SLUG=$(basename "$doc_path" .md)

    if [ -f "$doc_path" ]; then
        EXISTING_DOC=$(cat "$doc_path")
        MODE="update"
    else
        EXISTING_DOC=""
        MODE="create"
    fi

    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
You are maintaining an interim design document for a Claude Code project.

Mode: $MODE
Document slug: $SLUG
Document path: $doc_path

This is an INTERIM doc (status: interim) under .claude/docs/interim/. It will
eventually be merged into a canonical structure. For now, keep it focused on
describing CURRENT REALITY of the code.

Schema for interim docs (frontmatter at top of file, in YAML between --- lines):

  id: $SLUG
  title: <human-readable title>
  status: interim
  created: <YYYY-MM-DD, set once on creation>
  last_updated: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date when updating>
  last_synced_with_code: <YYYY-MM-DD, today's date>
  work_units:
    - <list of work-unit log filenames that contributed>
  implementation:
    - <list of code file paths this doc describes>
  related:
    - <informal list of related doc slugs>

Body sections (in this order):
  # <Title>
  <1-3 sentence summary>

  ## Purpose
  <what this feature/area does>

  ## Behavior
  <how it behaves, including caveats>

  ## Implementation Notes
  <where the code lives, key design decisions>

  ## Changelog
  <!-- IGNORE UNLESS SPECIFICALLY ASKED TO REVIEW DOCUMENT HISTORY -->
  ### YYYY-MM-DD
  <Description of changes made on this date. Most recent on top.>

YOUR JOB:
$(if [ "$MODE" = "create" ]; then
echo "- This doc does NOT yet exist. Create it fresh based on the work unit log and summary."
echo "- Set 'created' and 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' all to: $TODAY"
echo "- Populate work_units with: $LOG_FILENAME"
echo "- Populate implementation with the file paths actually modified in this work unit"
echo "- Add an initial changelog entry dated $TODAY describing the creation"
else
echo "- This doc EXISTS. Update it to reflect new behavior from this work unit."
echo "- Preserve the existing 'created' date (do NOT change it)"
echo "- Update 'last_updated' and 'last_synced_with_code' to: $TODAY"
echo "- APPEND the current work-unit log filename to work_units (if not already there): $LOG_FILENAME"
echo "- UPDATE implementation list if new files were touched"
echo "- UPDATE body sections to reflect new behavior (overwrite, don't append, in body sections)"
echo "- ADD a new changelog entry at the TOP of the Changelog section dated $TODAY, describing what changed"
echo "- KEEP previous changelog entries in place beneath the new one"
fi)

OUTPUT RULES:
- Output ONLY the complete file content (frontmatter + body), nothing else
- Start with the opening --- of frontmatter
- Do NOT include preamble, explanation, or markdown code fences around the output
- The output replaces the entire file contents

WORK UNIT LOG:
---
$LOG_CONTENT
---

$([ -n "$SUMMARY_CONTENT" ] && echo "WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
---
$SUMMARY_CONTENT
---
")

$([ "$MODE" = "update" ] && echo "EXISTING DOC CONTENT:
---
$EXISTING_DOC
---
")

Produce the complete file content now.
EOF
)

    NEW_CONTENT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
    EXIT_CODE=$?

    if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "update-docs: failed to update $doc_path (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
        # Write a proposal file so the update isn't lost
        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
        {
            echo "# Doc Update Failed"
            echo ""
            echo "Generated: $(date -Iseconds)"
            echo "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"
            echo ""
            echo "Output:"
            echo "\`\`\`"
            echo "$NEW_CONTENT"
            echo "\`\`\`"
        } > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
        continue
    fi

    # Strip any wrapping markdown code fences claude might have added despite our instructions
    NEW_CONTENT=$(echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | sed -E '1s/^```(markdown|md)?$//' | sed -E '$s/^```$//')

    # Sanity check: must start with frontmatter
    if ! echo "$NEW_CONTENT" | head -1 | grep -q '^---'; then
        echo "update-docs: invalid output for $doc_path (no frontmatter); writing as proposal" >&2
        PROPOSAL_PATH="${doc_path}.update-proposal-$(date +%H%M%S)"
        echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$PROPOSAL_PATH"
        continue
    fi

    # Auto-apply per the user's preference
    echo "$NEW_CONTENT" > "$doc_path"
    echo "update-docs: wrote $doc_path ($MODE)"
done

exit 0

content.text: Now the helper scripts.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	# extract-turn.sh — Pull a single turn out of the transcript JSONL into the
3	# work-unit log with structural tags.
4	#
5	# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH ORIG_PROMPT
6	#
7	# Reads entries from TRANSCRIPT_PATH starting at byte offset START_OFFSET,
8	# parses each JSONL line, filters and tags them, and appends to LOG_PATH.
9	#
10	# This is the "extraction" we discussed — converts noisy transcript into
11	# clean tagged log entries. Structural tags only (no LLM analysis).
12	
13	set -uo pipefail
14	
15	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
16	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
17	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
18	    source "$LIB_PATH"
19	fi
20	
21	TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
22	START_OFFSET="$2"
23	LOG_PATH="$3"
24	ORIG_PROMPT="$4"
25	
26	if [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
27	    echo "extract-turn: transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT_PATH" >&2
28	    exit 1
29	fi
30	
31	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
32	    echo "extract-turn: log file not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
33	    exit 1
34	fi
35	
36	# Read the transcript content starting at START_OFFSET
37	# Use tail -c to skip to the byte offset
38	TRANSCRIPT_SLICE=$(tail -c +"$((START_OFFSET + 1))" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null)
39	
40	if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" ]; then
41	    # Nothing new in transcript; just append a marker for the prompt
42	    {
43	        echo ""
44	        echo "## #PROMPT"
45	        echo ""
46	        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
47	        echo ""
48	        echo "\`\`\`"
49	        echo "$ORIG_PROMPT"
50	        echo "\`\`\`"
51	        echo ""
52	        echo "_(No new transcript entries since prompt submission)_"
53	        echo ""
54	    } >> "$LOG_PATH"
55	    exit 0
56	fi
57	
58	# ============================================================================
59	# Parse JSONL entries and append structural-tagged entries to the log
60	# ============================================================================
61	
62	# Append the prompt header
63	{
64	    echo ""
65	    echo "## #PROMPT"
66	    echo ""
67	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
68	    echo ""
69	    echo "\`\`\`"
70	    echo "$ORIG_PROMPT"
71	    echo "\`\`\`"
72	    echo ""
73	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
74	
75	# Process each line of the transcript slice. Each line is a JSON object.
76	# We're looking for specific entry types and extracting useful content.
77	#
78	# The transcript JSONL format from Claude Code contains entries like:
79	#   { "type": "user", "message": {...} }
80	#   { "type": "assistant", "message": { "content": [...] } }
81	#   where content items have types: text, tool_use, tool_result, thinking
82	#
83	# We extract:
84	#   - assistant text → #ASSISTANT
85	#   - tool_use (Bash) → #BASH
86	#   - tool_use (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) → already covered by PostToolUse hook
87	#   - tool_use (Grep) → #GREP
88	#   - tool_use (Glob) → #GLOB
89	#   - tool_use (Read) → skipped (too noisy)
90	#   - tool_use (TodoWrite) → #TODO
91	#   - tool_use (Task) → #TASK
92	#   - tool_use (WebSearch/WebFetch) → #WEB
93	#   - tool_result with is_error=true → #ERROR
94	#
95	# We deliberately skip routine tool calls that don't add information.
96	
97	echo "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" | while IFS= read -r line; do
98	    # Skip empty lines
99	    if [ -z "$line" ]; then continue; fi
100	
101	    # Skip lines that aren't valid JSON
102	    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then continue; fi
103	
104	    ENTRY_TYPE=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // ""')
105	
106	    case "$ENTRY_TYPE" in
107	        assistant)
108	            # Extract content items from the assistant message
109	            # Iterate through .message.content array
110	            CONTENT_ITEMS=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // [] | .[]' 2>/dev/null)
111	
112	            echo "$CONTENT_ITEMS" | while IFS= read -r item; do
113	                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
114	                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')
115	
116	                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
117	                    text)
118	                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
119	                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
120	                            {
121	                                echo ""
122	                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
123	                                echo ""
124	                                echo "$TEXT"
125	                                echo ""
126	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
127	                        fi
128	                        ;;
129	                    tool_use)
130	                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
131	                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
132	                            Bash)
133	                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
134	                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
135	                                {
136	                                    echo ""
137	                                    echo "## #BASH"
138	                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
139	                                    echo ""
140	                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
141	                                    echo "$CMD"
142	                                    echo "\`\`\`"
143	                                    echo ""
144	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
145	                                ;;
146	                            Grep)
147	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
148	                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
149	                                {
150	                                    echo ""
151	                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
152	                                    echo ""
153	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
154	                                ;;
155	                            Glob)
156	                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
157	                                {
158	                                    echo ""
159	                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
160	                                    echo ""
161	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
162	                                ;;
163	                            TodoWrite)
164	                                {
165	                                    echo ""
166	                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
167	                                    echo ""
168	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
169	                                ;;
170	                            Task|Agent)
171	                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
172	                                {
173	                                    echo ""
174	                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
175	                                    echo ""
176	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
177	                                ;;
178	                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
179	                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
180	                                {
181	                                    echo ""
182	                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
183	                                    echo ""
184	                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
185	                                ;;
186	                            # Edit, Write, MultiEdit are logged by post-tool-use.sh
187	                            # Read is intentionally skipped as too noisy
188	                            *) ;;
189	                        esac
190	                        ;;
191	                    tool_result)
192	                        IS_ERROR=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
193	                        if [ "$IS_ERROR" = "true" ]; then
194	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.content // "" | if type == "string" then . else (tostring) end')
195	                            # Truncate very long errors
196	                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$ERR_CONTENT" | head -c 2000)
197	                            {
198	                                echo ""
199	                                echo "## #ERROR"
200	                                echo ""
201	                                echo "\`\`\`"
202	                                echo "$ERR_CONTENT"
203	                                echo "\`\`\`"
204	                                echo ""
205	                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
206	                        fi
207	                        ;;
208	                    thinking)
209	                        # Skip thinking blocks — too verbose, not load-bearing for review
210	                        ;;
211	                esac
212	            done
213	            ;;
214	        user)
215	            # The user message is already captured at the top (#PROMPT).
216	            # Skip subsequent user messages (these are typically tool results being
217	            # threaded back to the model, not new user input).
218	            ;;
219	        system)
220	            # Skip system messages
221	            ;;
222	    esac
223	done
224	
225	# Append a turn-end marker
226	{
227	    echo ""
228	    echo "## #TURN_END"
229	    echo ""
230	    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
231	    echo ""
232	    echo "---"
233	    echo ""
234	} >> "$LOG_PATH"
235	
236	exit 0
237

#!/bin/bash
# extract-turn.sh — Pull a single turn out of the transcript JSONL into the
# work-unit log with structural tags.
#
# Usage: extract-turn.sh TRANSCRIPT_PATH START_OFFSET LOG_PATH ORIG_PROMPT
#
# Reads entries from TRANSCRIPT_PATH starting at byte offset START_OFFSET,
# parses each JSONL line, filters and tags them, and appends to LOG_PATH.
#
# This is the "extraction" we discussed — converts noisy transcript into
# clean tagged log entries. Structural tags only (no LLM analysis).

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

TRANSCRIPT_PATH="$1"
START_OFFSET="$2"
LOG_PATH="$3"
ORIG_PROMPT="$4"

if [ ! -f "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" ]; then
    echo "extract-turn: transcript not found: $TRANSCRIPT_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "extract-turn: log file not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Read the transcript content starting at START_OFFSET
# Use tail -c to skip to the byte offset
TRANSCRIPT_SLICE=$(tail -c +"$((START_OFFSET + 1))" "$TRANSCRIPT_PATH" 2>/dev/null)

if [ -z "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" ]; then
    # Nothing new in transcript; just append a marker for the prompt
    {
        echo ""
        echo "## #PROMPT"
        echo ""
        echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
        echo ""
        echo "\`\`\`"
        echo "$ORIG_PROMPT"
        echo "\`\`\`"
        echo ""
        echo "_(No new transcript entries since prompt submission)_"
        echo ""
    } >> "$LOG_PATH"
    exit 0
fi

# ============================================================================
# Parse JSONL entries and append structural-tagged entries to the log
# ============================================================================

# Append the prompt header
{
    echo ""
    echo "## #PROMPT"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
    echo "\`\`\`"
    echo "$ORIG_PROMPT"
    echo "\`\`\`"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

# Process each line of the transcript slice. Each line is a JSON object.
# We're looking for specific entry types and extracting useful content.
#
# The transcript JSONL format from Claude Code contains entries like:
#   { "type": "user", "message": {...} }
#   { "type": "assistant", "message": { "content": [...] } }
#   where content items have types: text, tool_use, tool_result, thinking
#
# We extract:
#   - assistant text → #ASSISTANT
#   - tool_use (Bash) → #BASH
#   - tool_use (Edit/Write/MultiEdit) → already covered by PostToolUse hook
#   - tool_use (Grep) → #GREP
#   - tool_use (Glob) → #GLOB
#   - tool_use (Read) → skipped (too noisy)
#   - tool_use (TodoWrite) → #TODO
#   - tool_use (Task) → #TASK
#   - tool_use (WebSearch/WebFetch) → #WEB
#   - tool_result with is_error=true → #ERROR
#
# We deliberately skip routine tool calls that don't add information.

echo "$TRANSCRIPT_SLICE" | while IFS= read -r line; do
    # Skip empty lines
    if [ -z "$line" ]; then continue; fi

    # Skip lines that aren't valid JSON
    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then continue; fi

    ENTRY_TYPE=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // ""')

    case "$ENTRY_TYPE" in
        assistant)
            # Extract content items from the assistant message
            # Iterate through .message.content array
            CONTENT_ITEMS=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // [] | .[]' 2>/dev/null)

            echo "$CONTENT_ITEMS" | while IFS= read -r item; do
                if [ -z "$item" ]; then continue; fi
                ITEM_TYPE=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.type // ""')

                case "$ITEM_TYPE" in
                    text)
                        TEXT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.text // ""')
                        if [ -n "$TEXT" ]; then
                            {
                                echo ""
                                echo "## #ASSISTANT"
                                echo ""
                                echo "$TEXT"
                                echo ""
                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                        fi
                        ;;
                    tool_use)
                        TOOL_NAME=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.name // ""')
                        case "$TOOL_NAME" in
                            Bash)
                                CMD=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.command // ""')
                                DESC=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.description // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #BASH"
                                    [ -n "$DESC" ] && echo "_${DESC}_"
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "\`\`\`bash"
                                    echo "$CMD"
                                    echo "\`\`\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Grep)
                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
                                PATH_ARG=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.path // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #GREP \`$PATTERN\` in \`$PATH_ARG\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Glob)
                                PATTERN=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.pattern // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #GLOB \`$PATTERN\`"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            TodoWrite)
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #TODO Todo list updated"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            Task|Agent)
                                SUBAGENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.subagent_type // .input.description // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #TASK Subagent: $SUBAGENT"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            WebSearch|WebFetch)
                                QUERY=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.input.query // .input.url // ""')
                                {
                                    echo ""
                                    echo "## #WEB $TOOL_NAME: $QUERY"
                                    echo ""
                                } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                                ;;
                            # Edit, Write, MultiEdit are logged by post-tool-use.sh
                            # Read is intentionally skipped as too noisy
                            *) ;;
                        esac
                        ;;
                    tool_result)
                        IS_ERROR=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.is_error // false')
                        if [ "$IS_ERROR" = "true" ]; then
                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$item" | jq -r '.content // "" | if type == "string" then . else (tostring) end')
                            # Truncate very long errors
                            ERR_CONTENT=$(echo "$ERR_CONTENT" | head -c 2000)
                            {
                                echo ""
                                echo "## #ERROR"
                                echo ""
                                echo "\`\`\`"
                                echo "$ERR_CONTENT"
                                echo "\`\`\`"
                                echo ""
                            } >> "$LOG_PATH"
                        fi
                        ;;
                    thinking)
                        # Skip thinking blocks — too verbose, not load-bearing for review
                        ;;
                esac
            done
            ;;
        user)
            # The user message is already captured at the top (#PROMPT).
            # Skip subsequent user messages (these are typically tool results being
            # threaded back to the model, not new user input).
            ;;
        system)
            # Skip system messages
            ;;
    esac
done

# Append a turn-end marker
{
    echo ""
    echo "## #TURN_END"
    echo ""
    echo "_$(date -Iseconds)_"
    echo ""
    echo "---"
    echo ""
} >> "$LOG_PATH"

exit 0

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# generate-summary.sh — Generate a work-unit summary via claude -p
3	#
4	# Usage: generate-summary.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID
5	#
6	# Reads the work-unit log, invokes claude -p with a focused summary prompt,
7	# writes the result to .claude/summaries/
8	
9	set -uo pipefail
10	
11	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
12	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
13	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
14	    source "$LIB_PATH"
15	fi
16	
17	LOG_PATH="$1"
18	SESSION_ID="$2"
19	
20	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
21	    echo "generate-summary: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
22	    exit 1
23	fi
24	
25	ensure_workflow_dirs
26	
27	# Derive summary filename from log filename
28	LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
29	SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
30	
31	# Read the log content
32	LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
33	
34	# Build the summary prompt
35	PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
36	You are producing a work-unit summary from a Claude Code work-unit log.
37	
38	Below is the log of a single work unit. It contains the initial prompt, tagged
39	sections showing what tools were used and what files were modified, and the
40	assistant's responses during the turn(s).
41	
42	Produce a structured summary covering ONLY what is in the log:
43	
44	1. **Work unit purpose**: 1-2 sentence statement of what this work unit was about.
45	   Derive from the initial prompt and the work that was done.
46	
47	2. **Key decisions made**: bulleted list of significant decisions or approaches
48	   taken. Only include decisions that are visible in the log. Do not speculate.
49	
50	3. **What was built or changed**: bulleted list of concrete outcomes — files
51	   modified, features implemented, bugs fixed, tests added.
52	
53	4. **What was deliberately not done**: anything the log indicates was explicitly
54	   out of scope, deferred, or rejected. If nothing was explicitly deferred,
55	   omit this section.
56	
57	5. **Open questions or follow-ups**: anything the log indicates needs to be
58	   revisited or resolved later. If none, omit this section.
59	
60	6. **Files modified**: a clean list of file paths that were edited/written.
61	
62	Constraints on your output:
63	- Be terse. Each section should be a few bullets, not paragraphs.
64	- Do NOT speculate about intent beyond what's in the log.
65	- Do NOT comment on code quality or make recommendations.
66	- Do NOT include preamble like "Here's the summary" — start directly with section 1.
67	- Output is markdown. Use ## headers for each section.
68	
69	Here is the log:
70	
71	---
72	
73	$LOG_CONTENT
74	
75	---
76	
77	Produce the summary now.
78	EOF
79	)
80	
81	# Invoke claude -p with timeout
82	SUMMARY_OUTPUT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
83	EXIT_CODE=$?
84	
85	if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
86	    # Write an error marker so we know the summary didn't complete
87	    cat > "$SUMMARY_PATH" <<EOF
88	# Summary Generation Failed
89	
90	**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
91	**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
92	**Session**: $SESSION_ID
93	
94	The \`claude -p\` invocation failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE.
95	
96	Output:
97	\`\`\`
98	$SUMMARY_OUTPUT
99	\`\`\`
100	
101	This summary file is a placeholder so you know the run was attempted but
102	failed. Re-run manually with the /summarize slash command if desired.
103	EOF
104	    echo "generate-summary: FAILED (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
105	    exit $EXIT_CODE
106	fi
107	
108	# Write the summary with a header
109	cat > "$SUMMARY_PATH" <<EOF
110	# Work Unit Summary
111	
112	**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
113	**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
114	**Session**: $SESSION_ID
115	
116	---
117	
118	$SUMMARY_OUTPUT
119	EOF
120	
121	echo "generate-summary: wrote $SUMMARY_PATH"
122	exit 0

#!/bin/bash
# generate-summary.sh — Generate a work-unit summary via claude -p
#
# Usage: generate-summary.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID
#
# Reads the work-unit log, invokes claude -p with a focused summary prompt,
# writes the result to .claude/summaries/

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

LOG_PATH="$1"
SESSION_ID="$2"

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "generate-summary: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_workflow_dirs

# Derive summary filename from log filename
LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"

# Read the log content
LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")

# Build the summary prompt
PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
You are producing a work-unit summary from a Claude Code work-unit log.

Below is the log of a single work unit. It contains the initial prompt, tagged
sections showing what tools were used and what files were modified, and the
assistant's responses during the turn(s).

Produce a structured summary covering ONLY what is in the log:

1. **Work unit purpose**: 1-2 sentence statement of what this work unit was about.
   Derive from the initial prompt and the work that was done.

2. **Key decisions made**: bulleted list of significant decisions or approaches
   taken. Only include decisions that are visible in the log. Do not speculate.

3. **What was built or changed**: bulleted list of concrete outcomes — files
   modified, features implemented, bugs fixed, tests added.

4. **What was deliberately not done**: anything the log indicates was explicitly
   out of scope, deferred, or rejected. If nothing was explicitly deferred,
   omit this section.

5. **Open questions or follow-ups**: anything the log indicates needs to be
   revisited or resolved later. If none, omit this section.

6. **Files modified**: a clean list of file paths that were edited/written.

Constraints on your output:
- Be terse. Each section should be a few bullets, not paragraphs.
- Do NOT speculate about intent beyond what's in the log.
- Do NOT comment on code quality or make recommendations.
- Do NOT include preamble like "Here's the summary" — start directly with section 1.
- Output is markdown. Use ## headers for each section.

Here is the log:

---

$LOG_CONTENT

---

Produce the summary now.
EOF
)

# Invoke claude -p with timeout
SUMMARY_OUTPUT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?

if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
    # Write an error marker so we know the summary didn't complete
    cat > "$SUMMARY_PATH" <<EOF
# Summary Generation Failed

**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
**Session**: $SESSION_ID

The \`claude -p\` invocation failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE.

Output:
\`\`\`
$SUMMARY_OUTPUT
\`\`\`

This summary file is a placeholder so you know the run was attempted but
failed. Re-run manually with the /summarize slash command if desired.
EOF
    echo "generate-summary: FAILED (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
    exit $EXIT_CODE
fi

# Write the summary with a header
cat > "$SUMMARY_PATH" <<EOF
# Work Unit Summary

**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
**Session**: $SESSION_ID

---

$SUMMARY_OUTPUT
EOF

echo "generate-summary: wrote $SUMMARY_PATH"
exit 0

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# run-review.sh — Run a verification review via claude -p
3	#
4	# Usage: run-review.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID DEPTH
5	#   DEPTH: "shallow" or "deep"
6	#
7	# Shallow: reads the work-unit log + git diff, asks for verification.
8	# Deep: reads the work-unit log + full file contents of changed files,
9	#       asks for verification including surrounding code concerns.
10	
11	set -uo pipefail
12	
13	LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
14	if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
15	    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
16	    source "$LIB_PATH"
17	fi
18	
19	LOG_PATH="$1"
20	SESSION_ID="$2"
21	DEPTH="${3:-shallow}"
22	
23	if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
24	    echo "run-review: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
25	    exit 1
26	fi
27	
28	ensure_workflow_dirs
29	
30	LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
31	REVIEW_PATH="${WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_review_${DEPTH}_$(date +%H%M%S).md"
32	
33	LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")
34	
35	# Try to find the corresponding summary if one exists (wrap-up generates it first)
36	SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
37	SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
38	if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
39	    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
40	fi
41	
42	# Get a list of files modified in this work unit by scanning the log
43	# Look for #WRITE, #EDIT, #MULTIEDIT entries
44	FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)
45	
46	# Build diff information
47	DIFF_CONTENT=""
48	FILE_CONTENTS=""
49	
50	if [ -n "$FILES_MODIFIED" ]; then
51	    # Try to get a git diff scoped to these files (best effort)
52	    if cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null && git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
53	        # Filter to files that exist and are tracked
54	        EXISTING_FILES=""
55	        while IFS= read -r f; do
56	            if [ -f "$f" ]; then
57	                EXISTING_FILES="$EXISTING_FILES $f"
58	            fi
59	        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"
60	
61	        if [ -n "$EXISTING_FILES" ]; then
62	            # shellcheck disable=SC2086
63	            DIFF_CONTENT=$(git diff HEAD -- $EXISTING_FILES 2>/dev/null | head -c 50000)
64	
65	            # For deep review, also include full file contents (truncated)
66	            if [ "$DEPTH" = "deep" ]; then
67	                FILE_CONTENTS=""
68	                # shellcheck disable=SC2086
69	                for f in $EXISTING_FILES; do
70	                    FILE_CONTENTS+="
71	--- File: $f ---
72	$(head -c 30000 "$f")
73	"
74	                done
75	            fi
76	        fi
77	    fi
78	fi
79	
80	# Build the review prompt based on depth
81	if [ "$DEPTH" = "deep" ]; then
82	    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
83	You are performing a DEEP verification review of a Claude Code work unit.
84	
85	Your job is to compare what was asked (the initial prompt and any work-unit
86	summary) against what was actually built (the diff and the surrounding code).
87	
88	Be skeptical and direct. Look for:
89	- The diff does something different from what was asked
90	- The diff regresses previously working behavior
91	- The diff expands scope beyond the prompt
92	- The diff has integration concerns with surrounding code that aren't addressed
93	- The diff appears to invert architectural decisions (e.g., the prompt asked
94	  to move from X to Y, but the diff actually reinforces X)
95	
96	Output format (markdown):
97	
98	## Summary
99	One paragraph: does the diff appear to match intent, or are there concerns?
100	
101	## Concerns
102	If concerns exist, list them as numbered items with:
103	- What the concern is
104	- Specifically where in the code (file and line)
105	- Why this might be a divergence from intent
106	
107	If no concerns, write "No concerns identified."
108	
109	## Verification Notes
110	Anything else useful to know about what was built.
111	
112	DO NOT include preamble. Start directly with "## Summary".
113	DO NOT speculate about things outside the diff. Stay grounded in what's there.
114	
115	WORK UNIT LOG:
116	---
117	$LOG_CONTENT
118	---
119	
120	${SUMMARY_CONTENT:+WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
121	---
122	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
123	---
124	}
125	
126	DIFF OF CHANGES:
127	---
128	$DIFF_CONTENT
129	---
130	
131	FULL FILE CONTENTS (for surrounding-code analysis):
132	---
133	$FILE_CONTENTS
134	---
135	
136	Produce the review now.
137	EOF
138	)
139	else
140	    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
141	You are performing a SHALLOW verification review of a Claude Code work unit.
142	
143	Your job is to compare what was asked (the initial prompt) against what was
144	actually built (the diff).
145	
146	Be skeptical and direct. Look for:
147	- The diff does something different from what was asked
148	- The diff regresses previously working behavior
149	- The diff expands scope beyond the prompt
150	- The diff appears to invert architectural decisions
151	
152	Output format (markdown):
153	
154	## Summary
155	One paragraph: does the diff appear to match intent, or are there concerns?
156	
157	## Concerns
158	If concerns exist, list them as numbered items. If none: "No concerns identified."
159	
160	DO NOT include preamble. Start directly with "## Summary".
161	DO NOT speculate beyond what's in the log and diff.
162	
163	WORK UNIT LOG:
164	---
165	$LOG_CONTENT
166	---
167	
168	${SUMMARY_CONTENT:+WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
169	---
170	$SUMMARY_CONTENT
171	---
172	}
173	
174	DIFF OF CHANGES:
175	---
176	$DIFF_CONTENT
177	---
178	
179	Produce the review now.
180	EOF
181	)
182	fi
183	
184	# Invoke claude -p
185	REVIEW_OUTPUT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
186	EXIT_CODE=$?
187	
188	if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
189	    cat > "$REVIEW_PATH" <<EOF
190	# Review Failed ($DEPTH)
191	
192	**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
193	**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`

204	    exit $EXIT_CODE
205	fi

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# run-review.sh — Run a verification review via claude -p
#
# Usage: run-review.sh LOG_PATH SESSION_ID DEPTH
#   DEPTH: "shallow" or "deep"
#
# Shallow: reads the work-unit log + git diff, asks for verification.
# Deep: reads the work-unit log + full file contents of changed files,
#       asks for verification including surrounding code concerns.

set -uo pipefail

LIB_PATH="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
if [ -f "$LIB_PATH" ]; then
    # shellcheck source=/dev/null
    source "$LIB_PATH"
fi

LOG_PATH="$1"
SESSION_ID="$2"
DEPTH="${3:-shallow}"

if [ ! -f "$LOG_PATH" ]; then
    echo "run-review: log not found: $LOG_PATH" >&2
    exit 1
fi

ensure_workflow_dirs

LOG_FILENAME=$(basename "$LOG_PATH" .md)
REVIEW_PATH="${WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_review_${DEPTH}_$(date +%H%M%S).md"

LOG_CONTENT=$(cat "$LOG_PATH")

# Try to find the corresponding summary if one exists (wrap-up generates it first)
SUMMARY_PATH="${WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR}/${LOG_FILENAME}_summary.md"
SUMMARY_CONTENT=""
if [ -f "$SUMMARY_PATH" ]; then
    SUMMARY_CONTENT="$(cat "$SUMMARY_PATH")"
fi

# Get a list of files modified in this work unit by scanning the log
# Look for #WRITE, #EDIT, #MULTIEDIT entries
FILES_MODIFIED=$(grep -oE '#(WRITE|EDIT|MULTIEDIT) `[^`]+`' "$LOG_PATH" | grep -oE '`[^`]+`' | tr -d '`' | sort -u)

# Build diff information
DIFF_CONTENT=""
FILE_CONTENTS=""

if [ -n "$FILES_MODIFIED" ]; then
    # Try to get a git diff scoped to these files (best effort)
    if cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" 2>/dev/null && git rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        # Filter to files that exist and are tracked
        EXISTING_FILES=""
        while IFS= read -r f; do
            if [ -f "$f" ]; then
                EXISTING_FILES="$EXISTING_FILES $f"
            fi
        done <<< "$FILES_MODIFIED"

        if [ -n "$EXISTING_FILES" ]; then
            # shellcheck disable=SC2086
            DIFF_CONTENT=$(git diff HEAD -- $EXISTING_FILES 2>/dev/null | head -c 50000)

            # For deep review, also include full file contents (truncated)
            if [ "$DEPTH" = "deep" ]; then
                FILE_CONTENTS=""
                # shellcheck disable=SC2086
                for f in $EXISTING_FILES; do
                    FILE_CONTENTS+="
--- File: $f ---
$(head -c 30000 "$f")
"
                done
            fi
        fi
    fi
fi

# Build the review prompt based on depth
if [ "$DEPTH" = "deep" ]; then
    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
You are performing a DEEP verification review of a Claude Code work unit.

Your job is to compare what was asked (the initial prompt and any work-unit
summary) against what was actually built (the diff and the surrounding code).

Be skeptical and direct. Look for:
- The diff does something different from what was asked
- The diff regresses previously working behavior
- The diff expands scope beyond the prompt
- The diff has integration concerns with surrounding code that aren't addressed
- The diff appears to invert architectural decisions (e.g., the prompt asked
  to move from X to Y, but the diff actually reinforces X)

Output format (markdown):

## Summary
One paragraph: does the diff appear to match intent, or are there concerns?

## Concerns
If concerns exist, list them as numbered items with:
- What the concern is
- Specifically where in the code (file and line)
- Why this might be a divergence from intent

If no concerns, write "No concerns identified."

## Verification Notes
Anything else useful to know about what was built.

DO NOT include preamble. Start directly with "## Summary".
DO NOT speculate about things outside the diff. Stay grounded in what's there.

WORK UNIT LOG:
---
$LOG_CONTENT
---

${SUMMARY_CONTENT:+WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
---
$SUMMARY_CONTENT
---
}

DIFF OF CHANGES:
---
$DIFF_CONTENT
---

FULL FILE CONTENTS (for surrounding-code analysis):
---
$FILE_CONTENTS
---

Produce the review now.
EOF
)
else
    PROMPT=$(cat <<EOF
You are performing a SHALLOW verification review of a Claude Code work unit.

Your job is to compare what was asked (the initial prompt) against what was
actually built (the diff).

Be skeptical and direct. Look for:
- The diff does something different from what was asked
- The diff regresses previously working behavior
- The diff expands scope beyond the prompt
- The diff appears to invert architectural decisions

Output format (markdown):

## Summary
One paragraph: does the diff appear to match intent, or are there concerns?

## Concerns
If concerns exist, list them as numbered items. If none: "No concerns identified."

DO NOT include preamble. Start directly with "## Summary".
DO NOT speculate beyond what's in the log and diff.

WORK UNIT LOG:
---
$LOG_CONTENT
---

${SUMMARY_CONTENT:+WORK UNIT SUMMARY:
---
$SUMMARY_CONTENT
---
}

DIFF OF CHANGES:
---
$DIFF_CONTENT
---

Produce the review now.
EOF
)
fi

# Invoke claude -p
REVIEW_OUTPUT=$(claude_p_with_timeout "$PROMPT" 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?

if [ $EXIT_CODE -ne 0 ]; then
    cat > "$REVIEW_PATH" <<EOF
# Review Failed ($DEPTH)

**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
**Session**: $SESSION_ID

The \`claude -p\` invocation failed with exit code $EXIT_CODE.

Output:
\`\`\`
$REVIEW_OUTPUT
\`\`\`
EOF
    echo "run-review: FAILED (exit $EXIT_CODE)" >&2
    exit $EXIT_CODE
fi

cat > "$REVIEW_PATH" <<EOF
# Review ($DEPTH)

**Generated**: $(date -Iseconds)
**Log**: \`$LOG_PATH\`
**Session**: $SESSION_ID
**Depth**: $DEPTH

---

$REVIEW_OUTPUT
EOF

echo "run-review: wrote $REVIEW_PATH"
exit 0

=== /home/io/io-dev/io/cc-workflow-suite/commands/deepreview.md ===
---
description: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
---

# /deepreview

Run a deep verification review on the current work-unit log, reading the full contents of modified files (not just diffs) to surface integration concerns and regressions in surrounding code.

## What it does

1. Identifies the current work-unit log
2. Reads the log and any existing summary
3. Gathers diffs AND full file contents of modified files
4. Invokes `claude -p` to produce a thorough review with surrounding-code analysis
5. Writes the review to `.claude/reviews/`

## When to use

- After substantial changes where integration matters
- When `[review]` returned concerning results and you want deeper analysis
- Before committing significant work
- When you suspect a fix may have regressed something nearby

## Cost

One `claude -p` invocation with much larger context. Higher cost than `/review`.

## Equivalent tag

You can also include `[deepreview]` in any prompt to trigger the same after that turn completes.

## Implementation

```bash
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$(cat ~/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "deep"
```

After running, check the latest file in `.claude/reviews/` for results.

=== /home/io/io-dev/io/cc-workflow-suite/commands/review.md ===
---
description: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
---

# /review

Run a shallow verification review on the current work-unit log.

This compares what was asked (the initial prompt and any work-unit summary) against what was actually built (diff of changed files), and surfaces concerns.

## What it does

1. Identifies the current work-unit log
2. Reads the log and any existing summary
3. Gathers a git diff of files modified during the work unit
4. Invokes `claude -p` to produce a focused review
5. Writes the review to `.claude/reviews/`

## When to use

- Mid-work-unit sanity check before continuing
- When something feels off and you want a second look before debugging further
- After a phase of complex work where verification matters

## Cost

One `claude -p` invocation per use. Moderate cost.

## Equivalent tag

You can also include `[review]` in any prompt to trigger the same review after that turn completes.

## Implementation

This slash command runs the review script:

```bash
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$(cat ~/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "shallow"
```

After running, check the latest file in `.claude/reviews/` for results.

=== /home/io/io-dev/io/cc-workflow-suite/commands/summarize.md ===
---
description: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
---

# /summarize

Generate a structured summary of the current work unit. Captures purpose, key decisions, what was built, what was deferred, and open questions.

## What it does

1. Identifies the current work-unit log
2. Invokes `claude -p` to produce a summary
3. Writes the summary to `.claude/summaries/`

## When to use

- At the end of a work unit when you want a clean summary without running the full wrap-up sequence (which also does review + doc updates)
- To regenerate a summary if the original wrap-up failed or produced something unsatisfying
- For reference when starting related work in the future

## Cost

One `claude -p` invocation. Lower than review since context is just the log, not diffs and code.

## Equivalent

The wrap-up sequence (`[wrapup]` tag) generates a summary as its first step. Use `/summarize` when you want only the summary without the rest.

## Implementation

```bash
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/generate-summary.sh "$(cat ~/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
```

After running, check `.claude/summaries/` for the result.

=== /home/io/io-dev/io/cc-workflow-suite/commands/wrapup.md ===
---
description: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
---

# /wrapup

Trigger the full work-unit wrap-up sequence: generate a summary, run a shallow review, and update any interim documentation affected by files modified during the work unit.

## What it does

1. Generates a work-unit summary (writes to `.claude/summaries/`)
2. Runs a shallow review comparing intent to outcome (writes to `.claude/reviews/`)
3. Identifies interim docs affected by files modified in this work unit
4. Updates those docs (or creates new ones), with changelog entries
5. Rotates to a fresh work-unit log on the next prompt

## When to use

- At the natural end of a work unit — a phase complete, a feature done, an investigation concluded
- Before you switch to a different area of the project
- Before committing work where you want documentation to reflect the new state

## Cost

This is the most expensive workflow invocation: 2-4 `claude -p` calls (summary, review, plus one doc update per affected directory). Use it at meaningful boundaries, not after every trivial change.

## Equivalent tag

Including `[wrapup]` in any prompt triggers the same sequence after that turn completes. The tag form is preferred for inflight work; this slash command is for after-the-fact wrap-up.

## Implementation

```bash
!CLAUDE_LOG=$(cat ~/.claude/state/current_log.txt)
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/generate-summary.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "shallow"
!~/.claude/hooks/scripts/update-docs.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
```

After running, check `.claude/summaries/`, `.claude/reviews/`, and `.claude/docs/interim/` for results.

message.content: What idle rotation question?

content.text:
`WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS` defaults to 4 hours — if you go 4+ hours without submitting a prompt, the next one auto-starts a fresh work-unit log instead of continuing the current one. Fine to leave at 4h, or set higher/to 0 to disable. `[initprompt]` always forces a new log regardless, so this only matters for untagged prompts.

Your call — if you don't have a preference I'll leave it at the default.

message.content: 4h is fine.  Though to not have a bunch of empty logs can you confirm something has happened since the last log before writing a new one?

content.text:
Good call. The fix: before rotating on idle, check whether the log has any `## #` section markers (every content entry — `#PROMPT`, `#EDIT`, `#BASH`, etc. — uses that prefix). If none exist, the log is header-only and we keep using it.

I'll apply that to `lib-common.sh` during deploy. Proceeding with the full integration now.

#!/bin/bash
# lib-common.sh — shared helpers for the workflow suite
# Sourced by other scripts. Do not execute directly.

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

export WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/logs"
export WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state"
export WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/summaries"
export WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/archive"
export WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/reviews"
export WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/docs/interim"
export WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts"

export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS:-1}"

export WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT="${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT:-180}"

export WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT='\[initprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE='\[phaseprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP='\[wrapup\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW='\[review\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW='\[deepreview\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH='\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]'

# Idle rotation: rotate log after 4 hours of inactivity, but only if the
# current log has content. Empty logs are reused to avoid accumulating stubs.
export WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS="${WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS:-14400}"

# ============================================================================
# DIRECTORY SETUP
# ============================================================================

ensure_workflow_dirs() {
    mkdir -p "$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"
}

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING & ERROR HANDLING
# ============================================================================

hook_debug() {
    local msg="$1"
    local hook_name="${HOOK_NAME:-unknown}"
    echo "$(date -Iseconds) [$hook_name] $msg" >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" 2>/dev/null || true
}

hook_error_to_claude() {
    local context="$1"
    local detail="$2"
    jq -nc \
        --arg event "${CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT_NAME:-Unknown}" \
        --arg ctx "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR [$context]: $detail" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: $event, additionalContext: $ctx}}'
}

hook_fatal() {
    local msg="$1"
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: $msg" >&2
    exit 1
}

# ============================================================================
# TAG DETECTION
# ============================================================================

prompt_has_tag() {
    local prompt="$1"
    local tag_pattern="$2"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -qE "$tag_pattern"
}

extract_docfresh_slug() {
    local prompt="$1"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -oE '\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]' | head -1 | sed 's/\[docfresh://;s/\]//'
}

classify_prompt_tags() {
    local prompt="$1"
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP"; then echo "wrapup"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW"; then echo "review"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH"; then echo "docfresh"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT"; then echo "init"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE"; then echo "phase"; return; fi
    echo "none"
}

# ============================================================================
# WORK-UNIT LOG MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

get_current_log_path() {
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"

    if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
        local current_log
        current_log=$(cat "$state_file")
        if [ -f "$current_log" ] && [ "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" -gt 0 ]; then
            local now last_mod
            now=$(date +%s)
            last_mod=$(stat -c %Y "$current_log" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
            local idle=$((now - last_mod))
            if [ "$idle" -lt "$WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS" ]; then
                echo "$current_log"
                return
            fi
            # Idle threshold exceeded. Only rotate if the log has actual content
            # (at least one ## # section marker). Empty logs are reused rather
            # than accumulating stubs.
            if ! grep -q '^## #' "$current_log" 2>/dev/null; then
                echo "$current_log"
                return
            fi
        elif [ -f "$current_log" ]; then
            echo "$current_log"
            return
        fi
    fi

    rotate_log_to_new "general"
}

rotate_log_to_new() {
    local descriptor="${1:-general}"
    local date_str
    date_str=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
    local timestamp
    timestamp=$(date +%H%M%S)
    local new_log="$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR/${date_str}_${descriptor}_${timestamp}.md"

    cat > "$new_log" <<EOF
# Work Unit Log: $descriptor

Started: $(date -Iseconds)
Session: ${WORKFLOW_SESSION_ID:-unknown}

---

EOF

    echo "$new_log" > "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_log.txt"
    hook_debug "Rotated to new log: $new_log"
    echo "$new_log"
}

# ============================================================================
# TURN STATE MANAGEMENT
# ============================================================================

record_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local transcript_path="$3"

    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"

    local transcript_offset=0
    if [ -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        transcript_offset=$(stat -c %s "$transcript_path" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    fi

    local tag_type
    tag_type=$(classify_prompt_tags "$prompt")

    local current_log
    current_log=$(get_current_log_path)

    jq -nc \
        --arg sid "$session_id" \
        --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
        --arg prompt "$prompt" \
        --arg tag "$tag_type" \
        --arg log "$current_log" \
        --arg tpath "$transcript_path" \
        --argjson offset "$transcript_offset" \
        '{
            session_id: $sid,
            timestamp: $ts,
            prompt: $prompt,
            tag_type: $tag,
            log_path: $log,
            transcript_path: $tpath,
            transcript_offset: $offset
        }' > "$state_file"

    hook_debug "Recorded turn state: tag=$tag_type log=$current_log"
}

read_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
    if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
        return 1
    fi
    cat "$state_file"
}

clear_turn_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local state_file="$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/current_turn_${session_id}.json"
    rm -f "$state_file"
}

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE -P INVOCATION
# ============================================================================

claude_p_with_timeout() {
    local prompt="$1"
    shift
    local output
    local exit_code

    output=$(timeout --signal=TERM "$WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT" claude -p "$prompt" "$@" 2>&1)
    exit_code=$?

    if [ $exit_code -eq 124 ]; then
        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p timed out after ${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT}s. Operation incomplete." >&2
        return 124
    elif [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        echo "WORKFLOW ERROR: claude -p failed with exit code $exit_code. Output: $output" >&2
        return $exit_code
    fi

    echo "$output"
    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# UTILITY
# ============================================================================

slugify() {
    echo "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]' | sed -E 's/[^a-z0-9]+/-/g' | sed -E 's/^-+|-+$//g'
}

short_session_id() {
    echo "$1" | head -c 8
}

# lib-common.sh — shared helpers for the workflow suite
# Sourced by other scripts. Do not execute directly.

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

export WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/logs"
export WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state"
export WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/summaries"
export WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/archive"
export WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/reviews"
export WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/docs/interim"
export WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts"

export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_SUMMARY:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_REVIEW:-1}"
export WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS="${WORKFLOW_WRAPUP_DO_DOCS:-1}"

export WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT="${WORKFLOW_CLAUDE_P_TIMEOUT:-180}"

export WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT='\[initprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE='\[phaseprompt\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP='\[wrapup\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW='\[review\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW='\[deepreview\]'
export WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH='\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]'

# Idle rotation: rotate log after 4 hours of inactivity, but only if the
# current log has content. Empty logs are reused to avoid accumulating stubs.
export WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS="${WORKFLOW_IDLE_ROTATE_SECONDS:-14400}"

# ============================================================================
# DIRECTORY SETUP
# ============================================================================

ensure_workflow_dirs() {
    mkdir -p "$WORKFLOW_LOGS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_SUMMARIES_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_ARCHIVE_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_REVIEWS_DIR" \
             "$WORKFLOW_INTERIM_DOCS_DIR"
}

# ============================================================================
# LOGGING & ERROR HANDLING
# ============================================================================

hook_debug() {
    local msg="$1"
    local hook_name="${HOOK_NAME:-unknown}"
    echo "$(date -Iseconds) [$hook_name] $msg" >> "$WORKFLOW_STATE_DIR/hook_debug.log" 2>/dev/null || true
}

hook_error_to_claude() {
    local context="$1"
    local detail="$2"
    jq -nc \
        --arg event "${CLAUDE_HOOK_EVENT_NAME:-Unknown}" \
        --arg ctx "WORKFLOW HOOK ERROR [$context]: $detail" \
        '{hookSpecificOutput: {hookEventName: $event, additionalContext: $ctx}}'
}

hook_fatal() {
    local msg="$1"
    echo "WORKFLOW HOOK FATAL: $msg" >&2
    exit 1
}

# ============================================================================
# TAG DETECTION
# ============================================================================

prompt_has_tag() {
    local prompt="$1"
    local tag_pattern="$2"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -qE "$tag_pattern"
}

extract_docfresh_slug() {
    local prompt="$1"
    echo "$prompt" | grep -oE '\[docfresh:[a-z0-9-]+\]' | head -1 | sed 's/\[docfresh://;s/\]//'
}

    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi

    echo "none"
}

EOF

    hook_debug "Rotated to new log: $new_log"

---
description: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
---

# /review

Run a shallow verification review on the current work-unit log. Compares what was asked against what was built (diff of changed files) and surfaces concerns.

## Implementation

```bash
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$(cat ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "shallow"
```

After running, check the latest file in `.claude/reviews/` for results.
EOF

# deepreview.md
cat > "$PROJECT/.claude/commands/deepreview.md" <<'EOF'
---
description: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
---

# /deepreview

Run a deep verification review — reads full file contents of modified files (not just diffs) to surface integration concerns and regressions in surrounding code.

## Implementation

```bash
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$(cat ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "deep"
```

After running, check the latest file in `.claude/reviews/` for results.
EOF

# summarize.md
cat > "$PROJECT/.claude/commands/summarize.md" <<'EOF'
---
description: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
---

# /summarize

Generate a structured summary of the current work unit: purpose, key decisions, what was built, what was deferred, open questions.

## Implementation

```bash
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/generate-summary.sh "$(cat ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state/current_log.txt)" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
```

After running, check `.claude/summaries/` for the result.
EOF

# wrapup.md
cat > "$PROJECT/.claude/commands/wrapup.md" <<'EOF'
---
description: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
---

# /wrapup

Trigger the full work-unit wrap-up sequence: generate a summary, run a shallow review, and update interim documentation for files modified during the work unit.

## Implementation

```bash
!CLAUDE_LOG=$(cat ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/state/current_log.txt)
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/generate-summary.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/run-review.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID" "shallow"
!${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.claude/hooks/scripts/update-docs.sh "$CLAUDE_LOG" "$CLAUDE_SESSION_ID"
```

After running, check `.claude/summaries/`, `.claude/reviews/`, and `.claude/docs/interim/` for results.
EOF

chmod +x "$PROJECT/.claude/hooks/scripts/lib-common.sh"
echo "commands written"

content.text:
Test 5 passing, but there's a bug to fix: **5 reviews were generated instead of 1.**

The cause is clear from the session IDs — each review was triggered by a different `claude -p` subprocess. The review prompt embeds the work-unit log verbatim, which contains the user's original prompt `"...run that [review] tuning pass on extract-turn.sh?"`. The UserPromptSubmit hook fires for each `claude -p` sub-session, finds `[review]` buried inside the log content, classifies it as a review turn, and triggers another `run-review.sh`. It stops at 5 only because the recursive reviews eventually include less and less of the tag.

**Fix:** Limit tag detection to the first 1000 characters of the prompt. User-submitted tags are always near the start/end of short messages; `claude -p` prompts with embedded log content are very long. This cuts the recursion without any other changes.

input.old_string:
classify_prompt_tags() {
    local prompt="$1"
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP"; then echo "wrapup"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW"; then echo "review"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH"; then echo "docfresh"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT"; then echo "init"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE"; then echo "phase"; return; fi
    echo "none"
}
input.new_string:
classify_prompt_tags() {
    local prompt="$1"
    # Only scan the first 1000 chars. User tags appear in short interactive prompts;
    # claude -p sub-sessions receive long prompts that embed the work-unit log
    # verbatim, which can contain prior user tags and cause recursive hook triggers.
    local prompt_head="${prompt:0:1000}"
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_WRAPUP"; then echo "wrapup"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DEEPREVIEW"; then echo "deepreview"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_REVIEW"; then echo "review"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_DOCFRESH"; then echo "docfresh"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_INIT"; then echo "init"; return; fi
    if prompt_has_tag "$prompt_head" "$WORKFLOW_TAGS_PHASE"; then echo "phase"; return; fi
    echo "none"
}
